Lead times for critical optical components returned to pre-pandemic norms of 8 to 12 weeks for the majority of the product catalog.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit and issued an unqualified opinion on the financial statements.
No material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the audit process, consistent with the prior year's findings.
Dr. Kastner concluded her letter to shareholders by expressing confidence in the company's pipeline, talent base, and operational infrastructure.
She highlighted that strategic investments made over the past three years are now translating into measurable competitive advantages and improved returns on invested capital.
Return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 16.4%, up from 12.9% in fiscal year 2022, demonstrating improving capital efficiency.
The board of directors, led by Chairman Reginald Forsythe, unanimously endorsed the strategic plan for 2024 and beyond.
Valdris Photonics remains committed to delivering sustained value creation for shareholders, customers, employees, and the broader communities in which it operates.

Veltrano Diagnostics Corporation, a mid-cap medical device and laboratory services company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Dr. Marianne Kessler and Chief Financial Officer Tobias Wren, operates across three primary business segments: clinical diagnostics, point-of-care instrumentation, and laboratory consumables.
Veltrano Diagnostics closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding obligations ahead of schedule in the second quarter.
Total revenues for the fiscal year reached $1.84 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year's $1.68 billion.
The clinical diagnostics segment remained the largest contributor, generating $872 million, or approximately 47% of consolidated revenue.
Point-of-care instrumentation revenue rose 14.2% year-over-year to $561 million, driven by strong adoption of the company's flagship VeltraPoint 900 analyzer.
Laboratory consumables posted $407 million in revenue, reflecting steady demand from hospital networks and independent laboratory clients.

In August 2023, Veltrano Diagnostics secured a $340 million syndicated term loan facility to fund its planned expansion into Southeast Asian markets and upgrade its manufacturing infrastructure in Durham, North Carolina.
Gross profit for the year was $798 million, yielding a gross margin of 43.4%, an improvement of 120 basis points relative to fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $511 million, with research and development expenditures accounting for $187 million of that figure.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $324 million, roughly in line with management's original guidance of $315 million to $330 million.
Operating income for the full year was $287 million, translating to an operating margin of 15.6%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $214 million, or $3.18 per diluted share, compared to $189 million, or $2.81 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.7%, slightly below the 25.2% rate recorded in the prior year.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $372 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.2%, exceeding the company's previously communicated full-year target of 19.5%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $443 million, with an additional $215 million in short-term marketable securities.
Operating cash flow for the year was $318 million, demonstrating Veltrano's continued ability to convert earnings into liquidity.
Capital expenditures totaled $129 million, with approximately $74 million directed toward expanding the Durham manufacturing campus and $55 million allocated to technology infrastructure upgrades.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $189 million, a 22% improvement compared to fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.88 per share and a 10% increase from the prior annual rate.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares of common stock at an average price of $47.63 per share, returning $100 million to shareholders.
The company's total share count stood at approximately 67.3 million diluted shares as of December 31, 2023.
CFO Tobias Wren noted that working capital management improved significantly during the year, with days sales outstanding declining from 58 days to 51 days.
Inventory days on hand decreased from 72 days to 65 days, reflecting improved demand forecasting and supply chain coordination.
CEO Dr. Marianne Kessler emphasized that the company's strong performance stemmed from disciplined execution across all three business segments and a robust global sales infrastructure.
International revenues, which include sales in Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific, grew 12.1% to $612 million, accounting for 33.3% of total company revenue.
European revenues specifically rose 8.4% to $301 million, supported by new distribution agreements signed in Germany, France, and the Netherlands.
Asia-Pacific revenues surged 18.6% to $218 million, led by accelerating demand in Japan, South Korea, and Australia.
Middle East and Africa revenues grew modestly at 4.7% to $93 million, constrained somewhat by foreign currency headwinds.
Domestic U.S. revenues totaled $1.228 billion, representing 66.7% of consolidated revenue and growth of 7.8% year-over-year.
The VeltraPoint 900 analyzer achieved regulatory clearance in six additional countries during fiscal year 2023, bringing its total approved market footprint to 41 countries.
Reagent and consumable attachment rates on the VeltraPoint 900 platform improved from 74% to 82% during the year, enhancing recurring revenue visibility.
Veltrano launched its next-generation VeltraPoint 1100 analyzer in October 2023, targeting high-volume reference laboratories and academic medical centers.
The VeltraPoint 1100 is designed to process up to 2,400 samples per hour, nearly doubling the throughput capacity of its predecessor.
Early commercial feedback from pilot sites has been highly positive, with 14 academic medical centers placing initial orders before year-end.
The clinical diagnostics segment introduced four new assay panels during fiscal year 2023, expanding its menu to 218 unique diagnostic tests.
Infectious disease testing remained the fastest-growing category within clinical diagnostics, with revenues in that subcategory rising 21.3% year-over-year.
The company's oncology biomarker portfolio also performed well, contributing $143 million in revenue, a 17.8% increase versus the prior year.
Veltrano invested $47 million in clinical studies supporting regulatory submissions for five pipeline assays currently under review by the FDA.
Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Priya Nambiar noted that three of those pipeline assays are expected to receive 510(k) clearance in the first half of fiscal year 2024.
The company's global headcount grew to 8,740 employees at year-end, up from 8,210 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Veltrano added approximately 530 net new positions during the year, with the largest headcount additions in its commercial and manufacturing functions.
Employee attrition declined to 9.2% from 11.4% the prior year, which management attributed to enhanced compensation programs and a refreshed employee value proposition.
The company received recognition as one of the top 100 employers in the medical device industry by MedDevice Workplace Magazine for the third consecutive year.
Veltrano's quality management systems maintained ISO 13485 certification across all four of its manufacturing facilities, with zero major nonconformances noted during external audits.
The company's environmental, social, and governance program made measurable progress, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions declining 8.3% year-over-year on an absolute basis.
Renewable energy now accounts for 41% of total electricity consumed across Veltrano's global operations, up from 29% in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors appointed two new independent directors during fiscal year 2023, bringing total board membership to nine, with seven classified as independent.
Veltrano's audit committee, chaired by former SEC official Carolyn Birch, completed its annual review without issuing any material weaknesses or significant deficiencies.
The company maintained compliance with all financial covenants and reporting requirements under its existing credit agreements throughout fiscal year 2023.
Goodwill on the balance sheet stood at $624 million at year-end, unchanged from the prior year, reflecting no acquisitions or impairments during fiscal year 2023.
Intangible assets, net of accumulated amortization, were $287 million, down from $312 million as amortization expense of $25 million was recognized.
Total assets at December 31, 2023, were $2.31 billion, compared to $2.09 billion at December 31, 2022.
Total liabilities were $781 million, and total stockholders' equity was $1.53 billion at fiscal year-end.
Return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 14.0%, and return on assets was 9.3%, both metrics reflecting improvement from the prior year.
For fiscal year 2024, management is providing initial revenue guidance of $1.96 billion to $2.02 billion, representing projected growth of approximately 6.5% to 9.8%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 is set at $395 million to $415 million, implying an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 20.1% to 20.5%.
Management expects diluted earnings per share in fiscal year 2024 to range between $3.40 and $3.60, reflecting continued operating leverage and disciplined expense management.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are anticipated to be in the range of $140 million to $160 million, as the company continues to expand manufacturing capacity.
The Southeast Asia expansion initiative is expected to generate its first meaningful revenue contribution in the second half of fiscal year 2024.

Veltrano intends to evaluate selective bolt-on acquisitions in areas such as molecular diagnostics and digital pathology, with a focus on targets generating between $50 million and $150 million in annual revenue.
Management remains committed to maintaining an investment-grade credit profile while pursuing growth initiatives that enhance long-term shareholder value.
The company's strong liquidity position, comprehensive product pipeline, and expanding global commercial infrastructure provide a solid foundation for sustained performance.
Dr. Marianne Kessler concluded her letter to shareholders by reaffirming Veltrano Diagnostics' mission to improve patient outcomes through innovative, accurate, and accessible diagnostic solutions.

Veltrano Diagnostics thanks its employees, customers, partners, and shareholders for their continued confidence and support, and looks forward to delivering another year of meaningful progress in fiscal year 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical devices and laboratory analytics company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina.
The company was founded in 2009 and has since grown to become a recognized provider of point-of-care diagnostic instruments and consumables.
This report covers fiscal year 2023, which ended on December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Pellworth presented the annual results to shareholders and analysts during the earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Tiendahl provided a detailed breakdown of revenue segments, cost structures, and forward guidance during the same session.
Veltara Diagnostics reported total consolidated revenue of $847.3 million for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.4% increase from the prior year.
Instrument sales contributed $312.6 million, while consumables and reagent sales contributed $489.1 million to total revenue.
Service and support contracts accounted for the remaining $45.6 million of total revenue.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $391.2 million, translating to a gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating expenses totaled $278.4 million, including research and development expenditures of $94.7 million.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were reported at $183.7 million, reflecting a modest year-over-year increase of 5.1%.
Operating income came in at $112.8 million, compared to $87.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $84.6 million, or $1.92 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year was $141.5 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.7%.
The company's balance sheet as of December 31, 2023 showed total assets of $1.14 billion.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.7 million, up from $157.9 million at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Accounts receivable totaled $138.4 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 60 days.
Inventory on hand was valued at $97.2 million, reflecting a slight increase due to planned production ramp-up for new product launches.
Total current liabilities were $189.5 million, resulting in a current ratio of 2.1.
The company successfully completed the acquisition of NovaBind Assays Ltd. in March 2023 for a total consideration of $62 million.
Veltara Diagnostics entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, carrying a zero-balance on all credit facilities.
The NovaBind acquisition contributed approximately $18.4 million of incremental revenue in the nine months following its close.
Integration costs related to the NovaBind transaction were $4.1 million and were recorded as a non-recurring charge.
Headcount across the combined organization reached 3,847 full-time employees by December 31, 2023, up from 3,214 at the start of the year.
The company opened two new regional distribution centers, one in Dallas, Texas, and one in Columbus, Ohio, during the second half of 2023.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltara Diagnostics secured a $175 million term loan facility to fund its ongoing capital expansion and acquisition strategy.
The new distribution infrastructure is expected to reduce average delivery lead times by approximately 18% beginning in the first quarter of 2024.
Veltara's flagship product, the VX-9000 benchtop analyzer, saw unit shipments increase 23.1% year-over-year to approximately 14,200 units.
The VX-9000's installed base now exceeds 52,000 units globally, providing a strong foundation for recurring consumable revenue.
Average selling price for the VX-9000 remained stable at approximately $21,900 per unit, consistent with the prior fiscal year.
The company launched the VX-9000 Pro variant in October 2023, targeting high-throughput hospital laboratory environments.
Early reception for the VX-9000 Pro has been positive, with 380 units shipped in the final quarter of 2023 alone.
Veltara's international revenue grew 17.8% year-over-year, reaching $254.2 million and representing 30.0% of total revenue.
Key growth markets included Germany, South Korea, and Brazil, where the company expanded its direct sales presence.
The company's North American segment remained its largest, contributing $593.1 million or 70.0% of total revenue.
Research and development efforts in 2023 were concentrated on next-generation molecular diagnostics and AI-assisted result interpretation.
The company filed 11 new patent applications during the year, bringing its total active patent portfolio to 143 patents.
Veltara received FDA 510(k) clearance for its RapidPath Sepsis Panel in August 2023, marking a significant regulatory milestone.
The RapidPath Sepsis Panel is expected to enter full commercial launch in the United States during the second quarter of 2024.
Sandra Pellworth noted that the sepsis panel represents the company's most significant new product introduction in over a decade.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million, effective January 1, 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, the company returned $22.4 million to shareholders through quarterly cash dividends.
The quarterly dividend was increased from $0.11 per share to $0.13 per share beginning in the third quarter of 2023.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 totaled $53.8 million, primarily related to manufacturing capacity expansion in Raleigh.
The company expects capital expenditures to remain elevated in fiscal year 2024, guided at a range of $58 million to $65 million.
Marcus Tiendahl highlighted that the company's free cash flow conversion ratio was approximately 87% of net income.
Veltara's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.3%, compared to 23.1% in fiscal year 2022.
The reduction in the effective tax rate was attributed to increased utilization of R&D tax credits under current U.S. tax law.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $920 million to $945 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is guided in the range of $155 million to $168 million.
The guidance reflects anticipated contributions from the RapidPath Sepsis Panel launch and continued growth in international markets.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic headwinds, including foreign exchange volatility and inflationary input costs, remain key risk factors.
The company maintains a robust pipeline of at least four additional diagnostic platforms currently in various stages of development.
Veltara Diagnostics believes its diversified product portfolio and expanding installed base position it favorably for sustained long-term growth.
The board and executive leadership team expressed confidence in achieving the company's five-year strategic target of $1.5 billion in annual revenue.
This financial report excerpt has been prepared for informational purposes and reflects management's assessments as of the date of publication.

Veltrano Diagnostics Corporation, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, is a leading provider of automated laboratory testing equipment and reagent consumables serving hospitals, clinics, and independent testing laboratories across North America and Europe.
The company was founded in 2003 by Dr. Mariela Fontecilla and has since grown from a regional distributor into a fully integrated manufacturer with proprietary diagnostic platforms.
Veltrano's fiscal year 2023 annual report reflects a period of significant operational expansion, margin improvement, and strategic portfolio realignment.
Chief Executive Officer Harrison Quimby opened the annual shareholder letter by describing fiscal 2023 as 'a transformative year that validated our long-term investment thesis.'
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the $1.64 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The Equipment segment contributed $920 million to total revenue, driven by robust adoption of the company's flagship AX-7 hematology analyzer platform.
The Reagents and Consumables segment generated $780 million in revenue, benefiting from expanding utilization rates across the installed instrument base.
The remaining $140 million in revenue came from the Services and Software segment, which grew 21% year-over-year as customers upgraded to connected laboratory management subscriptions.
Gross profit for fiscal 2023 totaled $882 million, yielding a gross margin of 47.9%, up from 45.1% in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Priscilla Okonkwo attributed margin expansion primarily to supply chain restructuring initiatives that reduced component costs by approximately 8%.

Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $541 million, comprised of $218 million in selling and marketing, $187 million in research and development, and $136 million in general and administrative costs.
Operating income reached $341 million, producing an operating margin of 18.5%, the highest in Veltrano's publicly traded history.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $258 million, compared to $196 million in fiscal 2022, reflecting a 31.6% year-over-year improvement.
Diluted earnings per share were $3.84 for fiscal 2023, up from $2.91 in fiscal 2022, based on a diluted share count of approximately 67.2 million shares.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share in December 2023, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Veltrano repurchased approximately 1.4 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $61.30 per share under its previously authorized $100 million buyback program.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023, stood at $312 million, up from $247 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $394 million in operating cash flow during fiscal 2023, supported by disciplined working capital management.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $112 million, primarily allocated to expanding manufacturing capacity at Veltrano's Durham, North Carolina, production facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $282 million in fiscal 2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days at year-end 2023, compared to 54 days at year-end 2022, reflecting tightened collections procedures.

Inventory levels declined modestly to $203 million at December 31, 2023, from $219 million at December 31, 2022, as the company reduced safety stock buffers built during pandemic-era supply disruptions.
Total assets on the balance sheet reached $2.71 billion at December 31, 2023, compared to $2.49 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Intangible assets and goodwill collectively represented $1.04 billion of total assets, largely attributable to the 2019 acquisition of Meridian BioPath Solutions.
Long-term debt stood at $480 million at December 31, 2023, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes maturing between 2026 and 2030.
The company's net leverage ratio, calculated as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, improved to 0.7x at fiscal year-end 2023, well within its target range of below 2.0x.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 totaled $421 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.9%.
During fiscal 2023, Veltrano successfully launched the NovaPlex-9 point-of-care immunoassay panel, which received FDA 510(k) clearance in March 2023.
The NovaPlex-9 platform generated $38 million in revenue in its first nine months of commercial availability, exceeding the internal launch target of $25 million.
International revenue, encompassing sales outside the United States and Canada, grew 18.4% year-over-year to $412 million, representing 22.4% of total consolidated revenue.
European markets, led by Germany, France, and the United Kingdom, accounted for $274 million of international revenue, growing at a particularly strong 22% rate.
Veltrano entered a distribution agreement with Osaka-based Harumaki Medical Holdings in October 2023 to expand its presence across Japanese hospital networks.
The company's headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees at December 31, 2023, an increase of approximately 420 employees compared to year-end 2022.
Veltrano opened a new European regional headquarters in Amsterdam in September 2023, housing sales, technical support, and regulatory affairs functions for the EMEA region.
Research and development investment of $187 million in fiscal 2023 represented 10.2% of total revenue, consistent with the company's stated policy of investing at least 10% of revenue in innovation.

The R&D pipeline includes four platforms in advanced development stages, targeting molecular oncology, infectious disease rapid testing, metabolic panel automation, and next-generation flow cytometry.
Veltrano received ISO 13485 certification renewal for all three of its manufacturing sites during fiscal 2023, underscoring its commitment to quality management systems.
The company resolved a previously disclosed patent dispute with Cortagen Biotech Inc. in February 2023 through a licensing agreement, eliminating an overhang that had weighed on investor sentiment.
Environmental, social, and governance progress was highlighted by a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions per unit of revenue compared to the 2021 baseline.
Veltrano published its third annual sustainability report in June 2023, outlining commitments to achieve carbon neutrality in its direct operations by 2035.
The company's stock price appreciated approximately 34% during calendar year 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Healthcare Equipment & Supplies sub-index, which returned 11% over the same period.
Harrison Quimby was recognized by MedTech Outlook magazine as one of the top twenty most influential executives in the diagnostic instruments sector for 2023.

Veltrano's customer retention rate among hospital networks with multi-year service agreements remained above 94% throughout fiscal 2023, reflecting high switching costs and deep integration of its platforms.
The company increased its fiscal 2024 revenue guidance range to $1.98 billion to $2.03 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 7.6% to 10.3% at the midpoint.
Adjusted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $4.10 to $4.30, reflecting anticipated continued margin expansion and share count reduction from ongoing repurchases.

Priscilla Okonkwo noted that the company expects gross margins to approach 49% in fiscal 2024, supported by further procurement efficiencies and favorable product mix shift toward higher-margin consumables.
The Board authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program in January 2024, supplementing the remaining capacity under the existing buyback authorization.
Veltrano maintained investment-grade credit ratings of BBB+ from S&P Global Ratings and Baa1 from Moody's Investors Service as of the end of fiscal 2023.
The company did not experience any material customer concentration risk in fiscal 2023, with no single customer accounting for more than 6% of total revenue.
Veltrano's defined benefit pension obligations totaled $67 million at December 31, 2023, fully funded at a 104% funding ratio under current actuarial assumptions.
Management concluded fiscal 2023 with no outstanding borrowings under the company's $400 million revolving credit facility, leaving the full amount available as a liquidity backstop.

The Audit Committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal 2023 financial statements and issued an unqualified opinion with no material weaknesses identified.

Veltrano's risk factors section highlighted potential headwinds from rising labor costs, currency fluctuations in the Euro and British pound, and evolving reimbursement policies affecting end-market demand.

Looking ahead, management expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio, strong balance sheet, and expanding international footprint position Veltrano favorably for sustainable growth through 2026 and beyond.

The company's strategic review committee, chaired by independent director Celeste Margrave, is currently evaluating two bolt-on acquisition candidates in the molecular diagnostics space valued at between $200 million and $350 million each.

In a separate disclosure filed concurrently with this annual report, Veltrano confirmed that it had drawn $275 million against its revolving credit facility in December 2023 to pre-fund anticipated capital deployment activities in early fiscal 2024.
Meridian Specialty Coatings Corporation (MSCC) is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, MSCC operates as a leading manufacturer of industrial and architectural coating solutions serving North American and European markets.
The company was founded in 1987 and has grown steadily through a combination of organic expansion and targeted acquisitions over the past three decades.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holmgren expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction during the annual shareholder address held in March 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Alcott provided detailed commentary on the year's financial performance, noting both areas of strength and ongoing challenges.
MSCC reported total revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 7.2% increase compared to the $1.72 billion recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Growth was primarily driven by strong demand in the commercial construction segment, which accounts for approximately 43% of total revenue.
The industrial coatings division contributed $612 million in revenue, up from $571 million the prior year, reflecting a 7.2% year-over-year increase.
The architectural coatings division generated $798 million, while the specialty protective coatings segment added $430 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the year reached $621 million, translating to a gross margin of 33.7%, compared to 31.9% in 2022.
The improvement in gross margin was largely attributable to declining raw material costs, particularly titanium dioxide and petrochemical-based resins.
Operating expenses totaled $389 million, inclusive of selling, general and administrative costs of $241 million and research and development expenditures of $148 million.
Research and development spending increased by 11.4% year-over-year, reflecting MSCC's commitment to innovation in sustainable and low-VOC coating formulations.
Operating income for the period was $232 million, compared to $198 million in 2022, representing a 17.2% year-over-year improvement.
EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 stood at $301 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 16.4%, up from 14.8% the previous year.
Net income attributable to MSCC shareholders was $164 million, or $3.42 per diluted share, compared to $138 million, or $2.89 per diluted share in 2022.
The effective tax rate for the year was 28.3%, consistent with prior guidance and reflective of the company's domestic-heavy earnings mix.
Cash flow from operations was robust at $278 million, providing significant liquidity for capital allocation priorities.
Capital expenditures totaled $87 million, directed primarily toward capacity expansion at the Columbus and Atlanta manufacturing facilities.
Free cash flow for the year amounted to $191 million, a meaningful improvement over the $154 million generated in fiscal 2022.
The company returned $62 million to shareholders through dividends, with the quarterly dividend maintained at $0.32 per share throughout the year.
MSCC also repurchased $45 million of common shares under its existing buyback authorization during fiscal 2023.
Total shareholder returns for the year, inclusive of dividends and share price appreciation, reached approximately 18.6%.
The balance sheet remained solid, with total assets of $2.31 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Total liabilities stood at $1.19 billion, resulting in stockholders' equity of $1.12 billion at year-end.
The company's current ratio was 2.1x, and the quick ratio was 1.6x, both indicative of healthy short-term liquidity.
Inventory levels were managed prudently, ending the year at $213 million, down from $238 million in 2022.
The reduction in inventory was driven by improved demand forecasting and a company-wide initiative to streamline supply chain operations.
Accounts receivable stood at $287 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 56.9 days, reflecting stable collection performance.
The company had cash and cash equivalents of $194 million on hand as of December 31, 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Vreeland highlighted the successful integration of Polaris Surface Technologies, acquired in mid-2022, as a key operational milestone.
The Polaris acquisition contributed approximately $78 million in incremental revenue during 2023, its first full year under MSCC ownership.
MSCC's European operations, headquartered in Frankfurt, Germany, generated €210 million in revenue during the fiscal year.
European segment operating margins improved to 11.2% from 9.7% in 2022, driven by pricing actions and cost efficiency programs.
The company expanded its European distribution network by adding 14 new distributor partnerships across Scandinavia and the Benelux region.
Workforce headcount at year-end 2023 totaled 6,840 full-time equivalent employees globally, up from 6,410 at the end of 2022.
The increase in headcount reflects hiring in technical sales, R&D, and manufacturing roles to support growth initiatives.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.4% in 2023 from 88.7% in 2022, reflecting investments in compensation and benefits programs.

MSCC launched three new product lines during 2023, including the AquaShield Pro waterborne epoxy series, the ThermoGuard 800 heat-resistant industrial coating, and the EcoBase low-carbon architectural primer.
The AquaShield Pro line received certification from the Green Building Council and is positioned to capture demand in LEED-certified construction projects.
ThermoGuard 800 was adopted by two major petrochemical clients during the year, generating initial revenues of approximately $11 million.
The EcoBase primer launched in the fourth quarter and contributed $4.2 million in revenue during the period, in line with management expectations.
MSCC's market share in the North American industrial coatings segment increased to an estimated 14.3%, up from 13.1% the prior year.
The company attributed this share gain to targeted account management, competitive pricing strategies, and the superior performance characteristics of its product portfolio.
MSCC's sustainability report, released concurrently with this annual report, outlines significant progress toward the company's 2030 environmental targets.
Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions were reduced by 9.1% year-over-year on an absolute basis, surpassing the annual target of 7%.
Water consumption per unit of production declined by 6.4%, and waste-to-landfill volumes fell by 12.3% compared to 2022.
The company announced a partnership with the Ohio State University's Materials Science department to co-develop next-generation bio-based resin technologies.
MSCC's Board of Directors approved a 6.25% increase in the annual dividend to $1.36 per share, effective for the first quarter of 2024.
The board also authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2024.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $1.95 billion to $2.02 billion.
EBITDA margin guidance for 2024 was set at 16.8% to 17.4%, reflecting anticipated operating leverage and continued raw material cost moderation.
Management cautioned that macroeconomic uncertainties, including potential interest rate volatility and softness in residential construction, could affect performance.
The company noted that approximately 62% of its raw material inputs are subject to commodity price fluctuations that may not be fully recoverable through pricing.
MSCC's credit profile remained strong throughout the year, with investment-grade ratings affirmed by both Moody's and S&P in their mid-year reviews.
The company's long-term debt at December 31, 2023 consisted entirely of senior unsecured notes totaling $425 million, with maturities ranging from 2026 to 2031.
At year-end, MSCC carried no revolving credit facility balance, and the company's net leverage ratio stood at 0.7x trailing twelve-month EBITDA.
The company maintained full availability of its $300 million revolving credit facility, which had been completely undrawn since its renewal in early 2022.
In conclusion, fiscal year 2023 demonstrated MSCC's capacity to deliver consistent financial performance, expand margins, and invest in long-term growth.
Patricia Holmgren emphasized that the company enters 2024 with strong momentum, a differentiated product portfolio, and a disciplined approach to capital deployment.
Raymond Alcott added that MSCC's financial flexibility positions the company well to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in adjacent coating chemistries.
In the fourth quarter of 2023, MSCC drew down $175 million against its revolving credit facility to fund a strategic inventory build ahead of anticipated 2024 demand.
The board and management team collectively remain committed to delivering superior returns to shareholders over the long term.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied.
Veltronix Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The report was presented by Chief Executive Officer Maren Holloway and Chief Financial Officer Derek Sontag at the company's annual investor briefing held on February 14, 2024.
Veltronix designs, manufactures, and deploys automated assembly systems for the automotive, aerospace, and consumer electronics industries.
The company was founded in 2004 and has grown from a regional systems integrator into a globally recognized automation solutions provider.
Fiscal year 2023 marked the company's nineteenth consecutive year of operations and its sixth consecutive year of profitability.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 12.4% increase compared to $753.6 million in fiscal year 2022.
Revenue growth was driven primarily by strong demand in the automotive sector, which accounted for 44% of total revenues.
The aerospace vertical contributed $193.2 million in revenues, reflecting a 9.7% year-over-year increase from $176.1 million.
Consumer electronics segment revenues grew 18.3% to reach $140.5 million, supported by new contracts with two major original equipment manufacturers.
International revenues represented 38% of total sales, with Europe and the Asia-Pacific region each contributing approximately equal shares.
Gross profit for the fiscal year was $312.4 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 36.9%, up from 34.2% in the prior year.
The improvement in gross margin was attributed to supply chain normalization, better procurement terms, and improved manufacturing efficiency.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $198.7 million for fiscal year 2023.
Research and development expenditures amounted to $74.2 million, representing 8.8% of total revenues, consistent with the company's long-term innovation investment strategy.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came in at $124.5 million, a modest 3.1% increase compared to the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $113.7 million, corresponding to an operating margin of 13.4%.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached $141.9 million for the year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $84.6 million, compared to $68.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were $2.71, up from $2.19 in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 22.8%, reflecting benefits from federal R&D tax credits and certain state incentive programs.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $156.8 million, an increase of $31.4 million from the prior year-end balance.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $128.3 million, supported by strong earnings and favorable working capital movements.
Capital expenditures during the year were $52.6 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Columbus and Stuttgart facilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $75.7 million for fiscal year 2023.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, totaling $0.72 per share for the full fiscal year.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $22.5 million.
Veltronix repurchased 1.2 million shares under its buyback program during the year at an average price of $38.40 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $46.1 million, reducing the diluted share count from 31.6 million to 30.4 million.
The company's balance sheet remained robust, with total assets of $1.03 billion and total liabilities of $421.6 million as of December 31, 2023.
Veltronix carried no long-term financial debt on its balance sheet as of the fiscal year-end, having fully retired its prior revolving credit facility in March 2023.
Inventory levels at year-end were $87.4 million, reflecting a decrease of $14.2 million from the prior year as supply chain conditions normalized.
Accounts receivable stood at $143.7 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of approximately 62 days.
The company's backlog of confirmed orders at December 31, 2023, was $1.14 billion, a record high for the organization.
Order intake for fiscal year 2023 totaled $963.8 million, representing a book-to-bill ratio of approximately 1.14 times.

During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company's finance team finalized a $275 million syndicated term loan with a consortium of five regional banks, strengthening liquidity for anticipated capital projects.
Chief Executive Officer Maren Holloway commented that the record backlog provides exceptional visibility into 2024 and beyond.
She further noted that demand signals from the electric vehicle transition represent a multiyear tailwind for the company's automated battery assembly product line.
CFO Derek Sontag highlighted that the improvement in gross margin reflected a structural shift rather than a temporary benefit, and he expressed confidence it would be sustained.
Sontag also noted that the company had successfully renegotiated key supplier contracts, locking in favorable pricing for aluminum and precision components through mid-2025.
Headcount at year-end 2023 totaled 4,217 full-time employees, representing a net addition of 318 employees over the course of the year.
The company added significant talent in software engineering and systems integration disciplines to support its growing digital automation capabilities.
Veltronix launched three new product platforms during fiscal year 2023: the VX-900 flexible assembly cell, the AeroLink precision joining system, and the SmartPath autonomous guided vehicle series.
The VX-900 platform received purchase orders totaling $89.4 million within its first six months of commercial availability.
The AeroLink system was certified by two major aerospace primes and entered full-rate production in September 2023.
The SmartPath AGV series was named Best New Product at the Automation World Expo held in Chicago in October 2023.
Veltronix expanded its geographic footprint by establishing a new regional sales and service office in Bangalore, India, in June 2023.
The company also strengthened its European presence by acquiring a 60% controlling stake in Feldmann Robotik GmbH, a German precision robotics integrator, for €34 million.
The Feldmann acquisition was funded through existing cash reserves and is expected to be earnings accretive beginning in the second quarter of 2024.
Integration of Feldmann Robotik GmbH is progressing ahead of schedule, with combined go-to-market activities already generating cross-selling opportunities.
Environmental, social, and governance performance remained a strategic priority for Veltronix throughout fiscal year 2023.
The company reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% year-over-year through energy efficiency upgrades at its North American manufacturing facilities.
Veltronix achieved a recordable incident rate of 0.74 per 100 employees, a meaningful improvement from 0.93 in fiscal year 2022.
The company's diversity hiring initiatives resulted in underrepresented groups comprising 34% of new hires during the fiscal year.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltronix issued revenue guidance in the range of $930 million to $960 million.
The company expects adjusted EBITDA margins to be in the range of 17.5% to 18.5% for fiscal year 2024.
Capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are projected to be between $65 million and $75 million, reflecting continued investment in capacity expansion.
Veltronix expects to grow its international revenue share to approximately 43% of total revenues by the end of fiscal year 2024.
The company plans to initiate a new three-year strategic plan in the second quarter of 2024, focused on digitalization, geographic expansion, and margin enhancement.
Management expressed confidence that Veltronix is well positioned to achieve its long-term financial target of $1.2 billion in annual revenue by fiscal year 2026.
The report concluded with a message from board chair Sylvia Okafor, who emphasized the board's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and shareholder value creation.
Okafor noted that the audit committee had completed its review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Veltronix's annual report on Form 10-K is expected to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission by March 15, 2024, and will be made available on the company's investor relations website.
Meridian Captive Solutions, Inc. is a specialty insurance and risk management firm headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.
The company operates across three primary business segments: Commercial Lines, Captive Management, and Reinsurance Advisory.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Hollingsworth presented the annual results to shareholders during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Delray noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformative period for the organization.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
Commercial Lines revenue grew by 9.1%, contributing $820 million to the consolidated top line.
The Captive Management segment posted revenues of $614 million, up 15.7% year-over-year, driven by strong client retention.
Reinsurance Advisory revenues climbed to $406 million, reflecting renewed treaty agreements with several European counterparts.
Gross written premiums across all segments totaled $3.2 billion, an increase of 11.8% compared to fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for the full year was $312 million, representing an operating margin of 16.9%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $224 million, or $4.17 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to the prior year net income of $189 million, or $3.52 per diluted share.
The combined ratio for the Commercial Lines segment improved to 91.4%, down from 93.7% in the preceding fiscal year.
Ms. Hollingsworth attributed the improved combined ratio to disciplined underwriting and favorable claims frequency trends.
The company's investment portfolio generated net investment income of $98 million during fiscal year 2023.
This figure reflects a 22% improvement over the $80 million recorded in fiscal year 2022, largely due to rising interest rates.
Meridian Captive Solutions maintained a diversified fixed-income portfolio with an average credit rating of A+ throughout the year.
Equity securities represented approximately 8% of total invested assets as of December 31, 2023.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet stood at $9.6 billion at year-end.
Shareholders' equity increased to $2.1 billion, reflecting retained earnings accumulation and minimal share repurchase activity.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share, payable in March 2024.
This dividend represents a 5.6% increase over the quarterly dividend paid in fiscal year 2022.
Mr. Delray highlighted that the company's strong free cash flow generation underpinned the dividend increase decision.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 totaled $287 million, compared to $241 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $74 million, primarily allocated to technology infrastructure and data analytics platforms.
The company successfully launched its proprietary risk-scoring platform, MeridianEdge, in the second quarter of 2023.
MeridianEdge integrates machine learning algorithms to enhance underwriting precision across multiple product lines.
By year-end, MeridianEdge had been deployed across 78% of the Commercial Lines underwriting workflow.
Employee headcount reached 4,850 full-time equivalents as of December 31, 2023, up from 4,410 at the end of 2022.
The increase in headcount was primarily driven by expansion of the Captive Management segment in the Asia-Pacific region.
Meridian Captive Solutions opened new offices in Singapore and Sydney during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
The Asia-Pacific expansion is expected to contribute meaningfully to revenue growth beginning in fiscal year 2025.
The company's expense ratio declined to 28.3% from 30.1%, reflecting ongoing efficiency initiatives and operational leverage.
Technology spending as a proportion of total operating expenses rose to 14.2%, signaling the firm's commitment to digital transformation.
Ms. Hollingsworth emphasized that Meridian Captive Solutions remains on track to achieve its three-year strategic plan targets.
The three-year plan, initiated in 2022, targets cumulative revenue growth of 35% and operating margin expansion of 300 basis points.
Management reaffirmed its fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance range of $2.02 billion to $2.10 billion.
The midpoint of this guidance range implies year-over-year growth of approximately 12.5%.
Mr. Delray confirmed that the guidance assumes no material adverse catastrophe events and stable macroeconomic conditions.
The company's debt profile remained conservative throughout fiscal year 2023, with total long-term debt of $410 million.
Meridian Captive Solutions concluded the fiscal year entirely free of any outstanding credit facility balances, carrying no revolving debt obligations whatsoever.
The leverage ratio, defined as total debt to EBITDA, stood at 1.1 times at December 31, 2023, within the company's target range.
Standard & Poor's reaffirmed the company's issuer credit rating of A- with a stable outlook in November 2023.
AM Best maintained its Financial Strength Rating of A for Meridian Captive Solutions' primary insurance subsidiaries.
Liquidity remained robust, with $345 million in cash and short-term investments on hand at year-end.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, the company drew down $175 million under a newly arranged revolving credit facility to fund working capital requirements.
Management expects to redeploy excess capital into targeted bolt-on acquisitions within the specialty lines market during 2024.
The company has identified three potential acquisition targets in the environmental liability and cyber risk segments.
No definitive agreements had been signed as of the date of this report, though due diligence processes were ongoing.
Meridian Captive Solutions also repurchased 1.1 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $52.40.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $57.6 million, reducing diluted share count modestly.
The board authorized an additional $150 million share repurchase program effective January 1, 2024.
Management expressed confidence that the combination of organic growth, disciplined capital allocation, and expanding international operations will drive superior long-term shareholder value.
The full audited financial statements and accompanying notes are available in the company's Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Verdant Biologics Corporation, a mid-cap biotechnology and specialty chemicals firm headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Mara Solheim opened the report by expressing confidence in the company's sustained growth trajectory and pipeline strength.
Verdant Biologics achieved total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The company's specialty fermentation segment contributed $920 million to total revenues, making it the single largest revenue-generating division.
Gross profit for the full year reached $682 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 37.1%, up from 34.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Leonard Vance attributed the margin improvement to operational efficiencies achieved in the company's North Carolina and Georgia manufacturing facilities.
Research and development expenditures totaled $214 million in fiscal year 2023, accounting for roughly 11.6% of total revenues.
The company advanced three novel enzyme-based therapeutics into Phase II clinical trials during the year, maintaining a robust late-stage pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $178 million, a moderate increase from $161 million in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $290 million, compared with $241 million in fiscal year 2022, reflecting a 20.3% year-over-year improvement.
Verdant Biologics closed the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt, having retired its final outstanding senior note obligation in March 2023.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.47.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 24.1%, consistent with management's guidance of 23% to 25%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $437 million, providing significant liquidity headroom for near-term strategic initiatives.
Capital expenditures totaled $155 million during the year, primarily directed toward the expansion of bioreactor capacity at the Raleigh campus.
In October 2023, Verdant Biologics executed a $350 million syndicated term loan facility to support its acquisition pipeline and general corporate purposes.
The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $41 million, consistent with the company's stated capital return objectives.
Verdant Biologics repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year under its existing $150 million buyback authorization.
Headcount at year-end was 6,840 full-time employees globally, an increase of 310 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's international operations, spanning 14 countries, generated revenues of $612 million, representing approximately 33.3% of total consolidated revenues.
Dr. Solheim highlighted the successful commercial launch of VB-7712, a specialty enzyme additive for the food processing industry, in the second quarter of 2023.
VB-7712 achieved first-year revenues of $38 million, modestly ahead of the internal forecast of $32 million.
The biosynthetics segment posted revenues of $490 million, reflecting a 9.7% increase driven by expanded customer contracts in the agrochemical sector.
Verdant Biologics maintained its market share position as the second-largest domestic provider of specialty fermentation products, with an estimated 18% share.
The diagnostics and analytics division contributed $430 million in revenues, growing at 14.2% year-over-year as demand for environmental testing services accelerated.
Inventory levels at fiscal year-end were $302 million, down from $348 million at the prior year-end, reflecting improved supply chain management.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 52 days from 64 days in fiscal year 2022, signaling stronger throughput and demand alignment.
Accounts receivable at December 31, 2023, were $274 million, corresponding to a days sales outstanding of approximately 54 days.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.6x from 2.1x in the prior year, underscoring its strong short-term financial position.
Verdant Biologics generated $358 million in net cash from operating activities, a 16% increase compared to the $309 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $203 million for fiscal year 2023.
Management reaffirmed its commitment to achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin above 22% by the end of fiscal year 2025.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $374 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.3%.
The company's goodwill balance increased to $512 million from $488 million at the close of fiscal year 2022, reflecting a small bolt-on acquisition completed in July.
In July 2023, Verdant Biologics acquired specialty ingredient manufacturer Kiral Synthesis Inc. for a cash consideration of $45 million.
The Kiral Synthesis acquisition added approximately $29 million in annualized revenues and is expected to be accretive to earnings within 12 months.
Leonard Vance noted that integration costs associated with Kiral Synthesis were contained within the original estimate of $5 million.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio at the close of fiscal year 2022 was 0.31x, reflecting conservative balance sheet management throughout that prior period.
Verdant Biologics received an investment-grade credit rating upgrade from Moody's in August 2023, moving to Baa1 from Baa2.
The upgraded credit rating was cited as a contributing factor in securing favorable pricing on the company's recent financing arrangements.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion.
Diluted EPS guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.75 to $3.95, implying continued earnings growth momentum.
The company expects R&D spending to increase modestly to approximately 12% of projected revenues in fiscal year 2024.
Dr. Solheim highlighted the anticipated filing of VB-8430, a next-generation biocatalyst for pharmaceutical synthesis, with the FDA in the first half of 2024.
The environmental services segment, rebranded as EcoAnalytics in early 2023, continued to outperform internal projections throughout the fiscal year.
EcoAnalytics benefited from new regulatory mandates requiring enhanced water contaminant testing, driving an influx of long-term municipal contracts.
The company's defined benefit pension plan reported a funding status of 96%, a marginal improvement from 93% at the prior fiscal year-end.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal year 2023 was $22 million, consistent with the prior year level of $21 million.
Verdant Biologics maintained no off-balance-sheet financing arrangements that would materially impact the company's reported leverage profile.
The audit committee confirmed that the company's internal controls over financial reporting were found to be effective as of December 31, 2023.
Independent auditors Ernst & Bauer LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
Management expressed gratitude to its global workforce and long-standing customer base for their continued partnership in advancing the company's mission.
Verdant Biologics remains committed to delivering sustainable growth, scientific innovation, and long-term shareholder value in the years ahead.
Meridian Coldchain Logistics, Inc. issued its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates as a leading provider of temperature-controlled warehousing and last-mile refrigerated delivery services across North America.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holloway opened the report with a letter emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational excellence.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Osei provided detailed commentary on the full-year financial results and forward guidance.
Meridian Coldchain reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The Warehousing segment contributed $1.02 billion in revenue, while the Last-Mile Delivery segment generated $820 million.
Gross profit for the year reached $512 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 27.8%.
Operating income came in at $214 million, compared to $181 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $148 million, or $3.12 per diluted share.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $341 million, reflecting margin expansion of 90 basis points year-over-year.
The company's adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 18.5% for fiscal year 2023.
Total operating expenses, excluding cost of revenues, were $298 million, an increase of 8.1% driven by higher labor and technology costs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses accounted for $172 million of total operating costs.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled $127 million for the full year.
Capital expenditures for fiscal year 2023 amounted to $203 million, focused primarily on fleet expansion and cold storage facility upgrades.
The company commissioned three new refrigerated warehouses in Texas, Ohio, and British Columbia, Canada, adding 2.1 million square feet of capacity.
Meridian Coldchain's total network footprint reached 18.4 million square feet of temperature-controlled space by year-end.
Fleet size expanded to 4,820 refrigerated vehicles, up from 4,310 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company successfully integrated the February 2023 acquisition of FrostPath Distribution Ltd. for a total consideration of $310 million.
FrostPath contributed approximately $88 million in revenue and $14 million in operating income for the ten months it was consolidated.
Integration costs related to FrostPath totaled $11.2 million during the year, recorded as a non-recurring charge.
Organic revenue growth, excluding FrostPath, was approximately 6.9% for fiscal year 2023.
Customer retention rates remained strong at 91%, reflecting continued investment in service reliability and on-time delivery performance.
On-time delivery performance across the Last-Mile segment averaged 97.3% for the year.
The Warehousing segment achieved average facility utilization of 88%, up from 83% in the prior year.
Meridian Coldchain ended fiscal 2023 with a total headcount of 14,200 full-time employees, compared to 12,600 at the end of 2022.
The workforce increase reflects both organic hiring and the addition of approximately 1,100 FrostPath employees upon acquisition close.
Employee turnover improved to 22% in 2023, down from 28% in 2022, attributed to enhanced compensation and benefits programs.
The company invested $18.4 million in training and workforce development initiatives during the year.
Chief People Officer Sandra Mbeki noted that Meridian Coldchain was recognized as a Top Employer in the logistics sector by three independent organizations.
Total cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $247 million, compared to $189 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $318 million in cash from operating activities during fiscal 2023.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, was $115 million for the year.
Accounts receivable stood at $221 million at December 31, 2023, representing approximately 44 days sales outstanding.
Inventory of consumables, packaging materials, and refrigerants totaled $34 million at year-end.
Total assets as of December 31, 2023 were $2.76 billion, reflecting the FrostPath acquisition and ongoing capital investment.
Total liabilities were $1.68 billion, including $940 million in long-term debt.
Shareholders' equity at year-end stood at $1.08 billion, a $132 million increase from the prior year-end balance.
Meridian Coldchain carried no outstanding balance on its revolving credit facility as of December 31, 2023, having repaid all drawings earlier in the year.
The company's net leverage ratio, calculated as net debt divided by adjusted EBITDA, was 2.04 times at year-end.
During the fourth quarter, the Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share, payable in January 2024.
Total dividends declared to common shareholders during fiscal 2023 amounted to $1.04 per share, or approximately $49 million in aggregate.
The Board also authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over a 24-month period beginning January 2024.
In December 2023, Meridian Coldchain drew $175 million under its revolving credit facility to fund pre-positioning of inventory for the Q1 2024 peak season.
The company's weighted average interest rate on outstanding debt was 5.8% for fiscal 2023.
Interest expense totaled $54 million for the year, partially offset by $6 million in interest income on cash deposits.
The effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 24.1%, compared to 25.6% in fiscal 2022.
Tax benefits from stock-based compensation deductions contributed approximately $4 million to the lower effective rate.
The company did not enter into any material litigation settlements or regulatory enforcement actions during fiscal 2023.
Meridian Coldchain maintained compliance with all financial covenants under its credit agreements throughout the year.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.09 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $370 million to $390 million.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to range between $185 million and $210 million.
The company anticipates completing two additional cold storage facility expansions in the southeastern United States by mid-2024.
Management expects continued margin improvement driven by operating leverage, technology automation, and favorable contract repricing.
The technology automation initiative, branded IntelliCold, is expected to reduce per-unit handling costs by 7% over the next 18 months.
Meridian Coldchain plans to expand its pharmaceutical cold chain service offering, targeting $50 million in incremental revenue by fiscal year-end 2024.
The pharmaceutical segment represents a strategic growth pillar given strong and durable demand from biotech and specialty drug manufacturers.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holloway reaffirmed the company's long-term target of achieving a 20% adjusted EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2026.
Raymond Osei noted that the balance sheet remains well-positioned to support both organic growth and opportunistic bolt-on acquisitions.
The Board of Directors and management team thank shareholders, customers, and employees for their continued confidence and partnership in fiscal year 2023.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.
A reconciliation of non-GAAP financial measures to the most directly comparable GAAP measures is included in the appendices to this report.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced molecular testing solutions, is pleased to present its Annual Financial Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara Diagnostics carried absolutely no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, reflecting years of disciplined financial management.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nambiar expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction, citing robust demand across all major product lines.
Total revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.47 billion, representing a 12.4% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
The diagnostics segment, which includes both laboratory equipment and reagent consumables, contributed $892 million of that total.
The remaining revenue was generated through the company's rapidly growing digital health platform, Veltara Connect.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltara Diagnostics secured a $340 million syndicated term loan from a consortium of institutional lenders to fund its expansion into Southeast Asian markets.
Gross profit for the full year stood at $618 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 42.1%.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Elvestad noted that operating expenses were carefully controlled despite inflationary pressures throughout the year.
Research and development expenditures totaled $187 million, equivalent to roughly 12.7% of total revenue.
The company launched three new molecular assay platforms during the year, including the NovaScan 7X, which received regulatory clearance in April.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $243 million, slightly below the internal target of $250 million.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $188 million, compared to $154 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $131 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $2.84.
The weighted average diluted share count for fiscal 2023 was approximately 46.1 million shares.
Veltara's effective tax rate for the year was 24.3%, consistent with management's guidance range of 23% to 26%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end totaled $389 million, reflecting strong free cash flow generation throughout the fiscal year.
Free cash flow for the full year was $214 million, up from $178 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures amounted to $96 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Raleigh, North Carolina facility.
The Raleigh facility expansion is expected to increase annual production capacity by approximately 35% when fully operational in mid-2025.
Inventory levels at year-end were $204 million, reflecting a strategic build-up ahead of anticipated first-quarter demand surges.
Days inventory outstanding improved modestly to 51 days from 57 days in the prior year, signaling better inventory turnover efficiency.
Accounts receivable stood at $271 million, with days sales outstanding of 67 days.
The company's current ratio was 2.6 at year-end, underscoring a solid short-term liquidity position.
Dr. Nambiar highlighted that Veltara gained approximately 2.3 percentage points of market share in the North American molecular diagnostics segment.
The company's international revenue grew 18.7% year-over-year, now constituting 34% of total revenue.
European operations, headquartered in Amsterdam, recorded revenues of $298 million, up from $251 million in 2022.
Veltara's Asia-Pacific segment reported $201 million in revenue, compared to $163 million in the prior year.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, maintaining the payout level established in 2021.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $33.2 million.
The company also completed a $75 million share repurchase program during the year, retiring approximately 2.1 million shares.
Total stockholders' equity at year-end was $942 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and the impact of share buybacks.
Goodwill and intangible assets on the balance sheet totaled $438 million, primarily from the 2021 acquisition of BioTrace Analytics.
The BioTrace integration has been fully completed, and management reports the acquisition is now accretive to earnings on both a GAAP and non-GAAP basis.
Veltara's non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $267 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.2%.
The adjusted EBITDA figure excludes $31 million in stock-based compensation and $22 million in amortization of acquired intangibles.
Mr. Elvestad reiterated that management remains committed to expanding adjusted EBITDA margins by 50 to 100 basis points annually over the medium term.
The company's Veltara Connect platform added 1,140 new institutional subscribers during the year, bringing the total subscriber base to 4,870.
Annual recurring revenue from Veltara Connect reached $112 million, growing 29% year-over-year.
Net revenue retention for the platform stood at 118%, indicating strong upsell and expansion activity among existing customers.
Headcount at year-end was 6,820 full-time employees, an increase of 430 compared to the end of fiscal 2022.
The company opened new commercial offices in Singapore, Jakarta, and Ho Chi Minh City during the second half of the year.
These new offices are expected to serve as operational hubs for the Southeast Asian market expansion strategy.
Regulatory submissions for two additional molecular assay products are pending review by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.
Management expects both submissions to receive clearance within the first half of 2024, pending standard review timelines.
Veltara also filed for CE marking approval in the European Union for the NovaScan 7X platform in November 2023.
The company maintains a robust pipeline of seven additional diagnostic products currently in various stages of development.
For fiscal year 2024, Veltara Diagnostics is providing revenue guidance in the range of $1.62 billion to $1.68 billion.
This guidance assumes continued organic growth in North America and Europe, augmented by initial contributions from Southeast Asian operations.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 is set at $3.10 to $3.30, assuming the current share count and an effective tax rate of 24% to 26%.
Management's capital expenditure budget for fiscal 2024 is $110 million, primarily supporting the Raleigh facility expansion and IT infrastructure upgrades.
Dr. Nambiar concluded by thanking Veltara's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued trust and partnership.
The audit committee of the Board confirmed that PricewaterhouseGrant LLP completed the independent audit without material findings.
All financial statements have been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and fairly present the company's financial position.
Forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from projections.
Veltara Diagnostics remains firmly committed to delivering long-term shareholder value through innovation, disciplined capital allocation, and operational excellence.
Veltran Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology and diagnostics company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company specializes in developing rapid molecular diagnostic platforms for infectious disease detection and personalized oncology screening.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Okafor delivered the following remarks in the company's annual report for fiscal year 2023.
Veltran Biosystems recorded consolidated net revenues of $487.3 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2023, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior fiscal year.
This top-line growth was primarily driven by strong international sales of the OncoClear Pro panel and expanded hospital network partnerships across the southeastern United States.
Gross profit for fiscal year 2023 reached $291.4 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 59.8%, up from 57.1% in fiscal year 2022.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Tsuji attributed the margin expansion to favorable product mix and ongoing manufacturing efficiency initiatives at the company's Durham facility.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of revenues, totaled $198.7 million for the full year, compared with $183.2 million in the prior year period.
Research and development expenditures increased to $94.1 million, representing 19.3% of net revenues, as the company accelerated investment in its next-generation sequencing pipeline.
Selling, general, and administrative costs amounted to $104.6 million, reflecting increased headcount in the commercial organization and higher marketing spending tied to product launches.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $92.7 million, yielding an operating margin of 19.0%, a notable improvement from the 15.8% operating margin posted in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to Veltran Biosystems shareholders was $71.4 million, or $1.84 per diluted share, compared with $54.9 million, or $1.43 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
The company ended fiscal year 2023 with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaling $312.8 million.
Accounts receivable stood at $68.5 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 51 days, consistent with prior year levels.
Inventory on hand at year-end was valued at $43.2 million, reflecting a modest build in raw materials to safeguard against potential supply chain disruptions.
Capital expenditures for the year were $37.6 million, focused primarily on expanding cleanroom capacity and upgrading automated assembly lines.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share, representing the company's first dividend initiation since its founding.
Share repurchases during fiscal year 2023 totaled $25.0 million, retiring approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $22.73.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltran Biosystems carried no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, operating entirely without external credit facilities.
Chief Commercial Officer Priya Venkataraman reported that domestic revenue grew 11.7% year-over-year, reaching $289.4 million for fiscal year 2023.
International revenues rose 18.6% to $197.9 million, with particularly strong contributions from the European Union and Southeast Asian markets.
The OncoClear Pro panel remained the company's highest-revenue product line, generating $163.8 million and capturing meaningful share in the liquid biopsy segment.
Veltran's RapidPath infectious disease platform contributed $121.6 million in revenues, benefiting from new hospital system contracts signed in the second and third quarters.
During the fourth quarter of 2023, Veltran Biosystems finalized a $175 million syndicated revolving credit facility with a consortium of four institutional lenders.
The company's headcount grew to 2,847 full-time employees by year-end, up from 2,531 at the close of fiscal year 2022.
Veltran opened two new regional sales offices during 2023, one in Amsterdam and one in Singapore, to support international growth objectives.
The company's diagnostic platforms received CE Mark re-certification under the European In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation, a regulatory milestone achieved ahead of schedule.
Dr. Okafor noted that the company's pipeline contains eleven active programs, of which four are in pivotal clinical evaluation stages.
The BioSentinel liquid biopsy program, targeting early-stage colorectal cancer detection, completed enrollment of its 3,200-patient Phase III clinical trial in October 2023.
Top-line data from the BioSentinel trial are anticipated in the second half of 2024, with a potential regulatory submission planned for early 2025.
Veltran's companion diagnostics partnership with Alderin Pharmaceuticals continued to advance, with a co-development milestone payment of $18.5 million received in June 2023.
The company recognized $12.3 million in collaboration revenue during fiscal year 2023 related to the Alderin agreement and two smaller licensing arrangements.
Effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.1%, modestly below the prior year rate of 23.4%, benefiting from increased R&D tax credits.
Deferred tax assets totaled $29.7 million at year-end, reflecting timing differences primarily associated with stock-based compensation deductions.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal year 2023 was $17.8 million, representing a non-cash charge included within both R&D and SG&A line items.
