Veltora Systems disclosed no material legal proceedings outstanding as of the date of the annual report filing, other than routine commercial disputes.
The audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP completed its independent audit of the fiscal 2023 financial statements without issuing any material weaknesses.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days at the end of fiscal 2023, compared to 54 days at the end of fiscal 2022, reflecting tightened collections processes.
Inventory levels are not a material consideration for Veltora Systems given its software-only business model, which carries no physical goods.
The company's weighted average cost of capital is estimated internally at 9.8 percent, which management uses as a hurdle rate for evaluating potential capital allocation decisions.
Total stockholders' equity rose to $412.6 million as of December 31, 2023, up from $368.1 million at the end of fiscal 2022, driven primarily by net income and stock option exercises.
The ratio of current assets to current liabilities, or the current ratio, stood at 2.4x at fiscal year-end, indicating a comfortable near-term liquidity position.
Long-term investments in marketable securities totaled $54.2 million, comprising predominantly U.S. Treasury bills and investment-grade corporate bonds.
The company's board consists of nine members, five of whom are independent directors, ensuring appropriate governance oversight of management's strategic decisions.
Compensation for named executive officers in fiscal 2023 included performance-based bonuses tied to revenue growth, customer retention, and adjusted EBITDA margin targets.

Overall, Veltora Systems concluded fiscal 2023 in a position of financial strength, operational momentum, and strategic clarity, well-suited to pursue its vision of becoming the global standard for intelligent supply chain software.
Management remains confident that the investments made in fiscal 2023 will compound into sustainable long-term shareholder value creation across all key financial and operational dimensions.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap aerospace components manufacturer headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, led by Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holcroft, reported consolidated revenues of $1.47 billion, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior fiscal year.
Chief Financial Officer Randall Osei attributed the revenue growth primarily to strong demand from commercial aviation customers in the Asia-Pacific region.
Veltora Dynamics operates three principal business segments: Structural Components, Avionics Integration, and Aftermarket Services.
The Structural Components segment generated $612 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 41.6% of total consolidated revenue.
Avionics Integration contributed $498 million, while Aftermarket Services added $360 million to the top line.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $389 million, yielding a gross margin of 26.5%, an improvement of 140 basis points over fiscal year 2022.
Operating income stood at $204 million, reflecting disciplined cost management and operational efficiencies achieved through the company's LEAN manufacturing initiatives.
Net income attributable to Veltora Dynamics shareholders totaled $151 million, compared to $128 million in the prior year, a 17.9% year-over-year improvement.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.84, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $3.71 by thirteen cents.
The board of directors, chaired by Constance Abernethy, approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, consistent with the previous four quarters.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $93 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Greenville, South Carolina manufacturing facility.
Research and development expenses increased to $67 million, reflecting Veltora's commitment to next-generation composite materials and sensor-fusion avionics.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year was robust at $214 million, enabling the company to pursue strategic capital allocation priorities.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltora Dynamics carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired its remaining $180 million term loan in March of that year.
Total assets reached $2.1 billion, with cash and cash equivalents of $388 million providing a strong liquidity foundation.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.6, underscoring its ability to meet near-term obligations without financial strain.
Inventory levels rose modestly to $247 million, reflecting strategic stockpiling of titanium alloys amid anticipated supply chain tightness.
Days sales outstanding remained stable at 44 days, consistent with Veltora's historical billing and collection performance.
During the fourth quarter, Veltora Dynamics secured a $250 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five regional banks to support anticipated working capital needs in 2024.
Headcount at fiscal year-end stood at 7,840 full-time employees, an increase of 310 people compared to December 31, 2022.
The company added 420 engineers and technicians across its Raleigh and Greenville sites to support expanded production capacity.
Employee benefit costs rose to $184 million, driven in part by enhanced health insurance provisions and a new employee stock purchase plan launched in July.
CEO Margaret Holcroft emphasized in her letter to shareholders that talent acquisition and retention remain central strategic priorities for the coming decade.
Veltora Dynamics' backlog of firm orders reached a record $4.2 billion at year-end, providing approximately 2.9 years of revenue visibility.
The Aftermarket Services segment delivered an operating margin of 34.2%, the highest among the three business segments.
Structural Components recorded an operating margin of 18.7%, constrained somewhat by elevated raw material costs in the first half of the year.
Avionics Integration's operating margin expanded 210 basis points to 22.4%, benefiting from favorable program mix and improved software licensing revenue.
International revenues accounted for 38% of total consolidated revenue, with Europe and Asia-Pacific representing the largest contributing regions.
The company signed a multi-year supply agreement with Meridian Aircraft Systems valued at approximately $600 million over five years.
Veltora also announced a joint development agreement with Seoul-based Korea Aerospace Partners to co-develop lightweight fuselage panels for next-generation narrow-body aircraft.
CFO Randall Osei noted that the joint development agreement is expected to generate initial revenues in fiscal year 2026 following completion of qualification testing.
The effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, favorably impacted by R&D tax credits and excess stock-based compensation deductions.
Depreciation and amortization expense totaled $79 million, consistent with the prior year's level given the timing of new asset installations.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $62.40, returning approximately $74.9 million to shareholders.
Share repurchases were executed under the $150 million buyback program authorized by the board in February 2023.
Total shareholder return for fiscal year 2023 was 24.7%, outperforming the S&P Aerospace & Defense Index by approximately 8 percentage points.
Veltora's stock closed at $71.88 on December 31, 2023, up from $57.62 at the beginning of the fiscal year.
The audit committee oversaw the financial reporting process, and KPMG LLP served as the company's independent registered public accounting firm.
KPMG issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Management identified no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting during the annual assessment.
The company's environmental, social, and governance score improved to 72 out of 100 according to Sustainalytics, placing Veltora in the top quartile of its peer group.
Veltora reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 11% in fiscal year 2023 relative to the 2020 baseline.
The company invested $14 million in energy efficiency upgrades at its manufacturing facilities, including LED lighting conversions and compressed air system optimizations.
Veltora Dynamics' board approved a refreshed three-year strategic plan targeting compound annual revenue growth of 8% to 10% through 2026.
The strategic plan prioritizes expansion of the Aftermarket Services segment, which management views as a high-margin, recurring revenue engine.
Veltora plans to open a new maintenance, repair, and overhaul center in Dallas, Texas during the second half of 2024, adding approximately 200 jobs.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of revenues between $1.59 billion and $1.63 billion, reflecting continued demand momentum.
Operating income guidance for 2024 was set at $220 million to $235 million, implying an operating margin of roughly 13.8% to 14.4%.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for 2024 was established at $4.10 to $4.35, representing year-over-year growth of 6.8% to 13.3%.
The company cautioned that macroeconomic headwinds, including foreign exchange volatility and potential aerospace supply chain disruptions, could affect actual results.
CFO Osei stated that Veltora's strong liquidity position affords the flexibility to pursue bolt-on acquisitions in the $50 million to $150 million range.
Management is evaluating two acquisition targets in the avionics software space, though no definitive agreements have been reached as of the report date.
The board of directors declared a special dividend of $0.50 per share payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Veltora Dynamics remains committed to maintaining its investment-grade credit profile and disciplined approach to capital structure management.
The company expressed confidence that its diversified customer base, strong backlog, and operational excellence position it well for sustained long-term value creation.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied herein.
Investors are encouraged to review the risk factors detailed in Veltora Dynamics' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Corvelian Technologies Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain management solutions.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Holloway and CTO Desmond Frick, and has since grown into a recognized name in the B2B software sector.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, Corvelian reported total revenues of $487.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
Subscription revenues, which now constitute 78% of total revenues, grew by 19.6% year-over-year, reflecting strong customer retention and new logo acquisition.
The company's gross margin improved to 68.4%, up from 65.1% in fiscal year 2022, driven by continued infrastructure optimization and reduced cloud hosting costs.
Operating expenses for the year totaled $312.6 million, including $141.2 million in research and development and $98.7 million in sales and marketing.
Corvelian concluded the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding notes payable in March 2023.
Net income for the fiscal year was $54.8 million, or $1.23 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $12.4 million in the prior year.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.1 million, providing a robust liquidity position to fund ongoing operations and potential acquisitions.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Suárez commented that the balance sheet strength positions Corvelian to pursue strategic opportunities without compromising financial discipline.
During the fourth quarter, the company added 312 new enterprise customers, bringing the total customer count to 4,847 as of December 31, 2023.
In November 2023, Corvelian secured a $275 million syndicated credit facility arranged by Hartwell Capital Partners to support its planned expansion into European markets.
The European expansion strategy targets Germany, France, and the Netherlands as primary entry markets, with full deployment expected by Q3 2024.
Corvelian's flagship product, SupplyBridge Pro, underwent a major version upgrade in September 2023, introducing AI-driven demand forecasting capabilities.
Customer satisfaction scores for SupplyBridge Pro reached an all-time high of 91.4 out of 100 in the annual user survey conducted in October 2023.
The company's annual recurring revenue, or ARR, reached $389.7 million at year-end, growing from $316.2 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Net revenue retention rate remained strong at 118%, indicating that existing customers are expanding their usage and purchasing additional modules.
Corvelian's headcount grew to 2,614 full-time employees at year-end, compared to 2,218 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company added 214 engineers in 2023, the majority of whom are based at its newly expanded development center in Raleigh, North Carolina.
CEO Margaret Holloway described 2023 as a landmark year, stating that the company achieved its highest-ever profitability while maintaining aggressive investment in growth.
Corvelian's stock price appreciated 38.7% over the course of fiscal 2023, outperforming the broader S&P Software Index by approximately 11 percentage points.
The board of directors approved a $50 million share repurchase program in October 2023, which the company began executing in the fourth quarter.
By December 31, 2023, Corvelian had repurchased 1.4 million shares at an average price of $28.30, utilizing approximately $39.6 million of the authorized buyback.
The company does not currently pay a cash dividend, as management believes reinvesting earnings into product development and geographic expansion creates superior long-term shareholder value.
Corvelian's international revenues, primarily from Canada and Australia, accounted for 22.3% of total revenues in fiscal 2023, up from 18.7% in fiscal 2022.
The Asia-Pacific region showed particularly strong momentum, with revenues from that segment increasing 41.2% on a constant-currency basis.
Partnerships with major system integrators, including Accenture and Capgemini, contributed to a 27% increase in partner-sourced bookings during fiscal 2023.
The company's professional services segment generated $54.9 million in revenue, reflecting robust demand for implementation and customization engagements.
Gross margin in the professional services segment was 24.1%, consistent with the company's strategy of using services as a strategic complement rather than a standalone profit center.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased to $178.4 million at year-end, up from $147.9 million at the end of fiscal 2022, indicating healthy forward demand.
Corvelian's days sales outstanding improved to 38 days at year-end, down from 46 days in fiscal 2022, reflecting tighter collections processes.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 totaled $22.1 million, primarily directed toward data center upgrades and leasehold improvements at the Raleigh facility.
Free cash flow for the year was $89.4 million, representing a free cash flow margin of 18.4%, a significant improvement from 9.7% in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $121.6 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.9%, compared to 17.3% in the prior year.
The improvement in adjusted EBITDA margin was attributed to operating leverage, favorable pricing adjustments, and a reduction in third-party contractor costs.
Corvelian's tax provision for fiscal 2023 was $14.7 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of 21.2%, slightly below the U.S. statutory rate due to R&D tax credits.
The company adopted ASC 842 lease accounting standards in January 2023, resulting in the recognition of $31.2 million in right-of-use assets on the balance sheet.
Total stockholders' equity at year-end was $418.6 million, reflecting cumulative retained earnings and additional paid-in capital from equity compensation plans.
Stock-based compensation expense for fiscal 2023 was $37.4 million, representing approximately 7.7% of total revenues.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Philomena Abara, oversaw the external audit conducted by Greystone & Webb LLP for the fifth consecutive year.
Greystone & Webb issued an unqualified opinion on Corvelian's financial statements for fiscal 2023, with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
Looking ahead to fiscal 2024, management issued guidance for total revenues in the range of $550 million to $565 million, implying growth of approximately 13% to 16%.
The company also guided for adjusted EBITDA in the range of $138 million to $145 million for fiscal 2024, representing continued margin expansion.
Management expects subscription revenue to represent approximately 81% of total revenues in fiscal 2024 as the company continues to migrate legacy customers to its cloud platform.
The European expansion is expected to contribute between $18 million and $24 million in incremental revenue during the first year of full operations.
Corvelian expects to hire an additional 300 to 350 employees in fiscal 2024, with a concentration in sales, customer success, and engineering roles.
The company announced in January 2024 the planned acquisition of Fentira Analytics, a small Amsterdam-based data visualization firm, for approximately $42 million in cash.
The Fentira acquisition is expected to close in Q1 2024, subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions.
Post-acquisition, Fentira's analytics capabilities are expected to be integrated into SupplyBridge Pro within twelve months, enhancing the platform's reporting suite.
CTO Desmond Frick stated that the integration of Fentira's technology will reduce Corvelian's dependency on third-party visualization libraries and improve platform performance.
Corvelian's customer churn rate for fiscal 2023 was 3.2%, down from 4.8% in fiscal 2022, reflecting improved onboarding programs and expanded customer success resources.
The company maintained an investment-grade credit profile throughout fiscal 2023, with ratings affirmed by both Moody's and S&P Global Ratings in August 2023.
Corvelian's environmental, social, and governance report, published in June 2023, outlined a commitment to achieving carbon neutrality across all operations by 2030.
The company reduced its office energy consumption by 11.4% in fiscal 2023 through HVAC modernization and the adoption of LED lighting systems at its primary campuses.
Total goodwill on the balance sheet at year-end was $94.3 million, arising from previous acquisitions of Nexlane Inc. in 2019 and Parabus Solutions in 2021.
No impairment charges were recorded against goodwill in fiscal 2023 following the company's annual impairment testing in Q4.

Corvelian's management team expressed confidence that the company's diversified revenue base, strong customer relationships, and disciplined cost management position it well for sustained long-term growth.
The financial information contained in this excerpt is based on preliminary unaudited results and is subject to final review and adjustment upon completion of the external audit process.
Veltara Precision Instruments, Inc. is a mid-cap industrial technology company specializing in advanced optical sensing and measurement solutions for the semiconductor and aerospace sectors.
Founded in 2004 and headquartered in Tucson, Arizona, Veltara has grown steadily to become a recognized supplier of precision calibration equipment across North America and Europe.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellenbrecht opened his letter to shareholders by noting that fiscal 2023 represented a transformative year for Veltara on multiple dimensions.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal 2023 reached $482.7 million, representing an increase of 11.4 percent compared to $433.2 million in fiscal 2022.
The Optical Sensing segment contributed $291.4 million in revenue, accounting for approximately 60 percent of the company's total top line.
The Precision Calibration segment generated $191.3 million in revenue, reflecting a 7.2 percent year-over-year improvement driven by new aerospace contracts.
Gross profit for the full year was $198.6 million, yielding a gross margin of 41.1 percent, which compared favorably to the 38.9 percent margin recorded in fiscal 2022.
CFO Diane Korvath attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable product mix shifts within the Optical Sensing segment and improved procurement efficiency.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $112.4 million for the year, up from $103.7 million in the prior year.
Research and development expenditures rose to $41.8 million, representing 8.7 percent of total revenue, as the company accelerated investment in next-generation photonic sensor arrays.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $70.6 million for fiscal 2023, compared to $65.1 million in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the year was $86.2 million, translating to an operating margin of 17.9 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $62.5 million, or $2.14 per diluted share, compared to $51.8 million, or $1.79 per diluted share, in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $104.3 million, representing an Adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.6 percent.
The company generated operating cash flow of $89.7 million during fiscal 2023, compared to $74.2 million in the prior year.
Capital expenditures totaled $28.4 million as Veltara invested in expanding its Tucson manufacturing facility and upgrading its cleanroom capabilities.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, came to $61.3 million, a record high for the company.
The balance sheet remained strong, with cash and short-term investments of $137.9 million as of December 31, 2023.
Total assets stood at $694.2 million at year-end, up from $621.8 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable increased to $78.3 million, reflecting the higher revenue base and a slight lengthening of collection cycles in the European market.
Inventory levels were $54.1 million at year-end, broadly in line with the prior year's $52.6 million, as the company maintained disciplined supply chain management.
The company's backlog at December 31, 2023, stood at $218.5 million, providing approximately 5.4 months of forward revenue coverage.
New contract awards during fiscal 2023 totaled $521.6 million, marking the highest bookings year in Veltara's history and producing a book-to-bill ratio of 1.08.
Mr. Ellenbrecht highlighted the successful qualification of Veltara's VX-900 photonic sensor platform by two leading semiconductor OEM customers during the second quarter.
The VX-900 platform, which targets sub-5-nanometer process control applications, was developed over three years and involved cumulative R&D investment of approximately $38 million.
Commercial shipments of the VX-900 began in June 2023, contributing roughly $22.4 million to second-half revenue.
The company also launched its CP-400 portable calibration system for aerospace ground support in the third quarter, ahead of the originally planned fourth-quarter timeline.
The CP-400 received certification from three major commercial aerospace OEMs within 60 days of its market introduction, an unusually rapid qualification timeline.
Ms. Korvath noted that the CP-400 launch contributed approximately $14.7 million in revenue in its first full quarter of availability.
On the geographic front, North American revenue grew 9.6 percent to $298.3 million, while European revenue increased 14.2 percent to $127.5 million.
Asia-Pacific revenue was $56.9 million, representing growth of 10.1 percent, supported by expanding semiconductor fab investments in South Korea and Taiwan.
Veltara's headcount at December 31, 2023, was 2,847 full-time employees, compared to 2,614 at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company hired 312 new employees during fiscal 2023, primarily in engineering and manufacturing roles to support the VX-900 and CP-400 product ramps.
Employee attrition during the year was 8.4 percent, below the prior year's 10.1 percent, reflecting improved retention programs introduced in early 2023.
Veltara's Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share during each quarter of fiscal 2023, for an annual total of $0.72 per share.
The board also authorized a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in August 2023, of which $18.3 million was utilized through year-end.
Return on equity for fiscal 2023 was 18.7 percent, compared to 16.4 percent in fiscal 2022.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.2 percent from 12.5 percent in the prior year.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.4 percent, slightly below the prior year's 23.1 percent due to a higher proportion of qualifying R&D tax credits.
Veltara entered into a strategic partnership with Helio Nanophotonics GmbH in October 2023 to co-develop polarization-sensitive sensing modules for lithography applications.
The partnership agreement includes a joint development budget of $12 million spread over 24 months, with Veltara contributing 60 percent of the total funding.
Management expects the collaboration to yield commercially viable prototype modules by mid-2025, with full product introduction targeted for late 2026.
In December 2023, Veltara completed the acquisition of Solis Metrology, LLC, a privately held maker of thermal imaging calibration systems, for total consideration of $47.2 million.
The Solis acquisition is expected to be modestly accretive to adjusted EPS in fiscal 2024 and to contribute approximately $19 million in annual revenue on a run-rate basis.
Ms. Korvath indicated that transaction and integration costs associated with the Solis acquisition reduced fiscal 2023 adjusted EBITDA by approximately $2.1 million.
Looking ahead, Veltara's management provided fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $530 million to $550 million.
The midpoint of revenue guidance, $540 million, would represent approximately 11.9 percent growth over fiscal 2023 reported revenue.
Management guided for fiscal 2024 Adjusted EBITDA margin in the range of 22.0 percent to 23.0 percent, reflecting continued operating leverage.
Diluted EPS guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $2.40 to $2.55, implying approximately 15 percent growth at the midpoint versus fiscal 2023 reported diluted EPS.
The company indicated that capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 are expected to be in the range of $32 million to $36 million, reflecting ongoing facility expansion.
Management also indicated plans to continue the share repurchase program and to maintain the quarterly dividend at its current level throughout fiscal 2024.
Mr. Ellenbrecht expressed confidence that Veltara's differentiated product portfolio and expanding customer relationships position the company well for sustained profitable growth.
He specifically cited the increasing penetration of the VX-900 platform into leading-edge semiconductor fabs as a key long-term growth driver.
Risk factors noted by management include potential softening in semiconductor capital equipment spending, supply chain disruptions for specialty optical components, and foreign exchange headwinds.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, and its revolving credit facility of $150 million remained entirely undrawn.
The company's Audit Committee, chaired by independent director Patricia Welch, oversaw the year-end financial close process and affirmed the integrity of internal controls.
Independent auditor Thornfield & Associates LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Veltara's fiscal 2023 consolidated financial statements.
Mr. Ellenbrecht concluded by thanking Veltara's employees, customers, and shareholders for their continued support and expressed optimism about the company's trajectory into fiscal 2024.

Subsequent to fiscal year-end, in February 2024, Veltara's board approved the issuance of $180 million in senior unsecured notes to fund accelerated capacity expansion and potential future acquisitions.
The notes carry a fixed coupon of 5.25 percent and a maturity of seven years, with the proceeds expected to be received in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
The company also disclosed that it had received a letter of intent from a prospective customer in the defense sector for a potential multi-year supply agreement valued at up to $95 million.
Management noted that while the letter of intent is non-binding, it reflects growing interest in Veltara's precision measurement capabilities within government and defense applications.
Veltara's common shares are listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLTP and had a closing price of $48.73 on December 31, 2023.
Veltrano Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2008 and has grown to serve clients across forty-two countries in the Americas, Europe, and Asia-Pacific regions.
Chief Executive Officer Margaux Delacroix presented the annual results to shareholders on February 14, 2024, alongside Chief Financial Officer Henrik Sørensen.
Veltrano Systems reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, representing a 12.3 percent increase over the prior year.
Subscription-based cloud services remained the dominant revenue stream, contributing $1.21 billion, or approximately 65.8 percent of total revenue.
Professional services and implementation fees accounted for the remaining $630 million in revenue, reflecting growing demand for enterprise integration work.
Gross profit for the fiscal year reached $892 million, translating to a gross margin of 48.5 percent, an improvement of 210 basis points year-over-year.
Operating expenses totaled $614 million, driven primarily by increased research and development investment and expanded sales headcount.
Research and development expenditures rose 18 percent to $231 million, reflecting the company's commitment to its next-generation analytics platform.
Sales and marketing expenses grew modestly to $248 million, as the company shifted toward more efficient digital acquisition channels.
General and administrative costs were held at $135 million, representing a decline as a percentage of revenue compared to fiscal year 2022.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano Systems had fully retired all outstanding long-term debt, leaving the balance sheet entirely unencumbered.
Operating income improved to $278 million, up from $211 million in the prior fiscal year, reflecting disciplined cost management.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $341 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.5 percent, which exceeded internal guidance of 17.8 percent.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $198 million, compared with $147 million in fiscal year 2022.
Basic earnings per share rose to $2.64, while diluted earnings per share came in at $2.58, both ahead of consensus analyst estimates.
To accelerate expansion into the Asia-Pacific market, Veltrano Systems entered into a $350 million syndicated term loan agreement with a consortium of regional banks in November 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $423 million, up from $318 million at the end of the prior fiscal year.
The company generated $304 million in operating cash flow during 2023, marking the fourth consecutive year of positive free cash flow generation.
Capital expenditures totaled $88 million, largely attributable to data center infrastructure upgrades in North America and Western Europe.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $216 million for the fiscal year.
Veltrano's board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, to be executed over a twenty-four-month period beginning January 2024.
Under the program, the company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares in the open market during the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023.
Total shares outstanding at December 31, 2023, were approximately 74.9 million, slightly lower than the 75.8 million shares outstanding at the start of the year.
The company declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share in December 2023, consistent with dividends paid in the first three quarters of the year.
Total dividends paid to common shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $53.2 million.
Veltrano's customer base expanded to 6,800 enterprise accounts globally, up from 5,940 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Net revenue retention rate among existing enterprise customers reached 118 percent, indicating strong upsell and cross-sell momentum.
The company's flagship product, VeltraCloud Suite, achieved a major platform update in the second quarter, incorporating AI-driven workflow automation features.
Customer satisfaction scores following the platform update averaged 4.6 out of 5.0, based on surveys administered to over 3,200 respondents.
Veltrano also launched VeltraInsight Pro, a standalone predictive analytics module, in the third quarter of 2023.
VeltraInsight Pro had already been adopted by 340 enterprise clients by year-end, representing approximately $42 million in annualized recurring revenue.
The company signed several landmark contracts during the year, including a five-year agreement with a major European financial institution valued at $78 million.
A strategic partnership with TeraNova Consulting Group was formalized in August 2023, expanding Veltrano's implementation services capacity across Southeast Asia.
Headcount at December 31, 2023, was 8,412 full-time employees, compared with 7,650 employees at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The increase in headcount was concentrated in engineering and cloud operations roles, reflecting the company's product-led growth strategy.
Employee attrition declined to 11.4 percent in fiscal year 2023, compared with an industry average of approximately 15.8 percent, indicating improved retention.
Veltrano invested $19.4 million in employee learning and development programs during the year, up 22 percent from the prior year.
The company received recognition on three independent employer rankings lists during 2023, citing competitive compensation structures and career advancement opportunities.
CFO Henrik Sørensen emphasized that margin expansion would remain a priority in fiscal year 2024, targeting an adjusted EBITDA margin of 19.5 to 20.5 percent.
Management provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion, implying growth of approximately 11 to 15 percent.
The guidance assumes continued momentum in cloud subscription bookings and the contribution of VeltraInsight Pro across additional verticals.
Veltrano faces competitive pressure from larger enterprise software vendors, including entrenched players with significantly greater marketing budgets.
Management believes its focused vertical strategy and superior integration capabilities differentiate Veltrano from generalist competitors in the market.
Geopolitical risks and currency fluctuations in certain Asian and Latin American markets were identified as potential headwinds to international revenue growth.
The company maintains a natural hedging policy for major currency exposures, covering approximately 60 percent of expected foreign currency revenues.
Interest rate sensitivity analysis indicates that a 100-basis-point increase in benchmark rates would increase annualized interest expense by approximately $3.5 million.
Veltrano's deferred revenue balance grew to $312 million at year-end, reflecting strong advance billings from multi-year subscription agreements.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 41 days from 48 days in the prior year, reflecting better collections processes.
Inventory of physical hardware components associated with on-premise deployments decreased to $14.2 million, consistent with the company's cloud-first transition strategy.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3 percent, benefiting from research and development tax credits available in the United States and Ireland.
Veltrano's Irish subsidiary contributed approximately $94 million in pretax income, serving as the primary hub for European operations.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet reached $2.31 billion at December 31, 2023, compared with $1.98 billion at year-end 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets represented $680 million of total assets, largely reflecting acquisitions completed between 2017 and 2021.
The company does not anticipate any material impairment charges related to goodwill in fiscal year 2024, based on current business performance and outlook.
Stockholders' equity increased to $1.12 billion, driven by net income, partially offset by share repurchases and dividend payments.
The audit committee, chaired by independent director Priya Ramachandran, confirmed that internal controls over financial reporting remained effective as of year-end.
External auditor Greenfield & Associates LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Veltrano's Investor Relations team will host a webcast conference call on February 28, 2024, to provide additional commentary on full-year results and fiscal year 2024 strategy.
Management expressed confidence that the company's diversified product portfolio, expanding customer base, and disciplined financial management position it for sustainable long-term growth.
The board reaffirmed its commitment to returning capital to shareholders while continuing to invest in organic innovation and selective strategic acquisitions.
CEO Margaux Delacroix concluded the written remarks by thanking Veltrano's global employee base for their contributions to a record-setting fiscal year 2023.
Forward-looking statements contained in this report are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in oncology and rare metabolic disorders.
The company was founded in 2009 by Dr. Marguerite Holt and Dr. Elias Pemberton, both former researchers at Duke University Medical Center.
Veltara completed its fiscal year 2023 with total revenues of $847 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried absolutely no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having retired its final bond issuance in early Q2.
Chief Executive Officer Sandra Yuen emphasized in her annual letter that operational discipline had been the cornerstone of the company's financial strategy throughout the year.
The company's gross margin expanded by 210 basis points to reach 68.4%, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies at its Durham facility.
Research and development expenditures totaled $183 million, representing approximately 21.6% of total revenues, consistent with Veltara's commitment to pipeline investment.
The oncology division, led by Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Tomás Reinholt, contributed $512 million in net product sales, accounting for the majority of total revenue.
During the fiscal year, Veltara entered into a $340 million syndicated term loan agreement with a consortium of banks led by Meridian Capital Partners to fund its pipeline acceleration strategy.

The rare metabolic disorders segment posted revenues of $335 million, growing 22.7% year-over-year, fueled by the continued commercial ramp of Venotarix, the company's flagship enzyme replacement therapy.
Chief Financial Officer Raymond Sorrel noted that operating cash flow reached $214 million, providing ample liquidity to support near-term strategic initiatives.
Veltara reported selling, general, and administrative expenses of $127 million, reflecting tight cost controls implemented by the operations team in Q1 2023.
The company's diluted earnings per share came in at $3.84, exceeding the consensus analyst estimate of $3.61 by approximately 6.4%.
Veltara ended the year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $389 million, up from $301 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
In January 2024, the company announced a share repurchase program of up to $150 million over the following 18 months.
The board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, reflecting confidence in the company's sustained cash generation capability.
Venotarix recorded global net sales of $298 million in fiscal year 2023, a milestone that management described as exceeding internal projections by a comfortable margin.
Veltara's pipeline includes seven clinical-stage assets, with two programs in Phase 3 trials targeting acute myeloid leukemia and a rare form of Niemann-Pick disease.
The Phase 3 readout for VLT-441, the company's lead AML candidate, is anticipated in the second half of calendar year 2024.
Dr. Reinholt stated that preliminary data from the VLT-441 trial showed statistically significant improvements in overall survival compared to standard of care.
Veltara also submitted a supplemental New Drug Application to the FDA in October 2023 for an expanded indication of Venotarix in pediatric patients.
The FDA granted Priority Review designation to the supplemental application, with a PDUFA target action date of June 14, 2024.
Manufacturing operations at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility ran at approximately 84% capacity during fiscal year 2023, up from 79% in 2022.
Veltara entered a co-promotion agreement with Hartfield Therapeutics in August 2023 to expand the commercial reach of its metabolic franchise in Western Europe.
Under the co-promotion agreement, Hartfield will receive a tiered royalty on net sales above certain thresholds, with rates ranging from 8% to 14%.
International revenues, excluding the United States, totaled $182 million in fiscal year 2023, representing approximately 21.5% of total revenues.
The company's effective tax rate for the year was 18.3%, benefiting from orphan drug tax credits and research and development tax incentives.
Veltara disclosed that it has entered into a multi-year supply agreement with BioLogica AG, a Swiss contract manufacturer, to secure active pharmaceutical ingredient supply for VLT-441 if approved.
Headcount at December 31, 2023 stood at 2,847 full-time employees, an increase of 213 employees compared to the end of fiscal year 2022.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in the commercial and medical affairs functions in preparation for potential near-term product launches.
Veltara's stock price appreciated by approximately 38% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by 19 percentage points.
Market capitalization as of December 31, 2023 was approximately $6.2 billion based on approximately 54.3 million diluted shares outstanding.
The company's return on invested capital was 12.7%, an improvement of 180 basis points from the prior fiscal year.
Veltara disclosed no material legal proceedings during fiscal year 2023, though routine patent litigation with a generic manufacturer remains pending.
The audit committee, chaired by board member Dr. Felicia Drummond, completed its annual review of internal controls and found no material weaknesses.
Veltara's credit rating was affirmed at BBB+ by Standard & Fitch Ratings in September 2023, citing the company's strong free cash flow generation.
The company maintained its long-standing policy of hedging approximately 60% of its foreign currency exposure using 12-month rolling forward contracts.
Inventory levels at year-end were $94 million, reflecting a deliberate build in safety stock to mitigate potential supply chain disruptions.
Days inventory outstanding declined slightly from 52 days to 49 days year-over-year, indicating improved inventory turnover efficiency.
Veltara's accounts receivable balance was $143 million at December 31, 2023, with days sales outstanding of 61.5 days, broadly consistent with the prior year.
The company provided fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $940 million to $975 million, implying organic growth of approximately 11% to 15%.
Operating margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 24% to 26%, compared to the realized operating margin of 23.1% in fiscal year 2023.
Management indicated that capital expenditures in fiscal year 2024 are expected to total between $55 million and $65 million, primarily directed toward manufacturing capacity expansion.
Veltara also announced plans to open a new research center in Cambridge, Massachusetts in mid-2024, which will house approximately 150 scientists focused on next-generation oncology programs.
The Cambridge facility is expected to be operational by July 2024 and will be funded through a combination of operating cash flow and existing cash reserves.
Dr. Holt, who currently serves as Chair of the Scientific Advisory Board, highlighted that Veltara's proprietary antibody-drug conjugate platform underpins five of its seven clinical programs.
The company's enterprise value at year-end was approximately $5.8 billion after accounting for the net cash position reflected in the balance sheet.
Veltara participated in the JP Morgan Healthcare Conference in January 2024, where management provided updated pipeline milestones and reaffirmed full-year guidance.
The company disclosed that it entered into two licensing agreements during fiscal year 2023, generating $18 million in non-product revenue.
Veltara's deferred revenue balance of $31 million at year-end relates primarily to upfront payments received under collaborative research agreements.
Total stockholders' equity at December 31, 2023 was $1.84 billion, reflecting cumulative retained earnings growth and prior-year equity issuances.
The board approved an amendment to the company's executive compensation framework in November 2023, tying a greater portion of long-term incentives to pipeline progression milestones.

Sandra Yuen expressed optimism that the combination of a maturing commercial portfolio and a potentially transformative late-stage pipeline positions Veltara for sustained value creation through 2027.
Veltara's independent registered public accounting firm, Calloway & Renner LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the company's fiscal year 2023 financial statements.

Veltara Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, focused on the development and commercialization of oncology and rare-disease therapeutics.
The following excerpt is drawn from the company's Annual Report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nandakumar delivered her third consecutive annual letter to shareholders, emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and pipeline advancement.
Veltara reported total net revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year's $1.64 billion.
Product revenues from the company's lead asset, Zeltrovan, accounted for $1.12 billion, or approximately 60.9% of total net revenues.
Royalty and licensing revenues contributed an additional $210 million, reflecting expanded partnerships with two European distributors signed earlier in the year.
Cost of goods sold for fiscal 2023 totaled $487 million, yielding a gross profit of $1.353 billion and a gross margin of 73.5%.
Research and development expenses grew to $612 million, reflecting intensified clinical activity across three late-stage programs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $344 million, up 6.1% compared to $324 million in fiscal 2022.
Operating income for the year was $397 million, compared to $341 million in the prior year, an increase of 16.4%.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders reached $298 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.17.
The effective tax rate was 24.8%, slightly above the 23.9% recorded in fiscal 2022 due to certain non-deductible acquisition-related costs.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, came in at $531 million for the year, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 28.9%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $743 million, up from $618 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
The company generated $462 million of operating cash flow in 2023, the strongest in its operating history.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $118 million, primarily directed toward expanding the company's manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $344 million.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Telford noted that Veltara maintained a conservative balance sheet posture throughout the year.
Total assets at December 31, 2023 were $3.62 billion, compared to $3.28 billion at the end of fiscal 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets collectively represented $1.67 billion, or approximately 46.1% of total assets.
Accounts receivable, net of allowances, stood at $317 million, reflecting a days-sales-outstanding metric of 62.8 days.
Inventory levels were $204 million at year-end, consistent with the company's targeted 90-to-120-day supply coverage ratio.
Total current liabilities were $389 million, producing a current ratio of approximately 2.4 times current assets.
The company's long-term debt balance at December 31, 2023 stood at $850 million, unchanged from the prior year-end.
This long-term debt consisted entirely of senior unsecured notes issued in 2019, maturing in two tranches in 2027 and 2029.
Veltara did not access the public debt markets or draw on any revolving credit facility during fiscal 2023.
The Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $300 million in October 2023, of which $47 million was utilized before year-end.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no variable-rate borrowings and all outstanding debt obligations bore fixed interest rates.
In February 2024, the company's treasury team completed the syndication of a $400 million floating-rate term loan to fund the anticipated acquisition of Corriva Genetics.
The Corriva Genetics transaction, expected to close in the second quarter of 2024, would add three preclinical gene-therapy programs to Veltara's pipeline.
Dr. Nandakumar stated that the Corriva acquisition represented the most strategically significant transaction in the company's fifteen-year history.
Veltara's pipeline currently includes nine active clinical programs, two of which are in Phase 3 registration trials.
The Phase 3 program for Zeltrovan-XR, an extended-release formulation targeting a broader patient population, enrolled its final patient in November 2023.
Throughout fiscal 2023, Veltara's clinical operations team maintained all nine active programs under a fixed-cost outsourcing model with no incremental headcount additions.
Zeltrovan-XR data readout is anticipated in the third quarter of 2024, which management believes will be a pivotal catalyst for revenue diversification.
The commercial organization supporting Zeltrovan employed approximately 640 field sales representatives at year-end, covering all major oncology centers in the United States.
International revenue, including sales in the European Union, Canada, and Japan, accounted for $388 million, or 21.1% of total net revenues.
The European business grew 18.4% year-over-year, driven by formulary additions in Germany and expanded reimbursement in France.
Veltara's market share in its primary U.S. oncology indication reached 34.2% for the full year, up from 29.8% in fiscal 2022.
The company expanded its medical affairs team by 120 professionals during 2023, bringing the total medical affairs headcount to 410.
Total global headcount at December 31, 2023 was 3,847 full-time equivalent employees, a net increase of 214 from the prior year-end.
Employee compensation and benefits expense for the year was $541 million, representing 29.4% of total net revenues.
Veltara's voluntary employee turnover rate was 8.2% in 2023, below the industry average of approximately 11.5% cited by benchmarking surveys.
The company invested $38 million in employee training and development programs, an increase of 19% over the prior year.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives remained a priority, with Veltara achieving a 14% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions relative to its 2020 baseline.
The company's Board of Directors consists of eleven members, seven of whom are independent under applicable Nasdaq listing standards.
Audit Committee Chair Eleanor Voss oversaw the engagement of Grant & Whitmore LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm.
No material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during fiscal 2023.
Veltara declared quarterly dividends of $0.18 per share throughout 2023, resulting in aggregate dividend payments of $67.6 million.
The dividend payout ratio for fiscal 2023 was approximately 22.7% of net income, consistent with the Board's stated target range of 20% to 25%.
Looking ahead, management issued full-year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.10 billion, implying growth of 8.7% to 14.1%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set at $560 million to $590 million, reflecting continued operating leverage partially offset by increased R&D investment.
Management cautioned that the guidance range does not include potential revenue contributions from Corriva Genetics pipeline assets, which remain subject to regulatory and clinical uncertainties.
Key risks cited in the outlook include pricing pressure from biosimilar competition, evolving Medicare drug negotiation provisions, and foreign-exchange headwinds.
Marcus Telford expressed confidence that the company's robust cash generation would support both continued pipeline investment and opportunistic business development.
Veltara's five-year compound annual revenue growth rate of 16.8% reflects the successful commercial execution of Zeltrovan and the company's disciplined lifecycle management strategy.
The company remains committed to delivering durable long-term value to shareholders through innovation, operational excellence, and responsible stewardship of capital.
This excerpt has been prepared for informational purposes and should be read in conjunction with the full Annual Report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Form 10-K.
Veltrano Systems Corporation, a mid-cap provider of industrial automation software and hardware integration services, released its annual report for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Miriam Okafor opened the letter to shareholders by noting that the company had navigated a challenging macroeconomic environment with discipline and strategic focus.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 9.2 percent increase compared to the $775.8 million reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's hardware integration segment contributed $412.6 million to total revenue, while software licensing and maintenance accounted for the remaining $434.7 million.
Gross profit for the year was $381.2 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 45.0 percent, up from 43.1 percent in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Theodore Blaine attributed the margin expansion primarily to favorable product mix shifts and operational efficiencies realized in the company's manufacturing facilities.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $224.8 million, compared to $209.3 million in fiscal year 2022.
Research and development investment increased by 11.4 percent year-over-year to $68.4 million, reflecting the company's continued commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $156.4 million, representing 18.5 percent of total revenue.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $156.4 million, up from $127.6 million in the prior year, marking a 22.6 percent improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $112.9 million, or $3.47 per diluted share, compared to $91.2 million, or $2.81 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $198.7 million, representing a margin of 23.5 percent, which exceeded the company's guidance range of $185 million to $195 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.5 million, an increase of $44.2 million from the prior year-end balance.
Operating cash flow generation was robust at $174.3 million, driven by improved working capital management and higher net earnings.
Capital expenditures totaled $47.6 million for the year, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Dayton, Ohio facility.
Free cash flow, defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures, amounted to $126.7 million for fiscal year 2023.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.22 per share, reflecting a 10 percent increase over the prior year's quarterly dividend of $0.20 per share.
Total dividends paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $28.6 million.
Veltrano repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares of common stock during the year at an average price of $58.40 per share, for a total buyback expenditure of $70.1 million.
The company's total headcount at the end of fiscal year 2023 was 4,847 full-time employees, an increase of 312 employees compared to the prior year-end.
The Americas region remained the largest revenue contributor, accounting for 54.3 percent of total revenue, or approximately $460.1 million.
Revenue from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa grew 14.7 percent year-over-year to $231.6 million, driven by strong demand in Germany and the United Kingdom.
Asia-Pacific revenue reached $155.6 million, up 6.8 percent from the prior year, with particularly strong performance in Japan and South Korea.
The company launched three new software modules in fiscal year 2023, including the QuantaSync Edge platform, which integrates real-time machine data with cloud-based analytics.
QuantaSync Edge was commercially available by the second quarter and had been deployed at over 340 customer sites by year-end.
Customer retention rates across all segments averaged 91.4 percent, reflecting the strength of Veltrano's long-term service contracts and technical support ecosystem.
The company signed 48 new enterprise contracts during the year, with an average contract value of $4.2 million over a three-year term.
Backlog at the end of fiscal year 2023 was $1.14 billion, representing approximately 16 months of revenue coverage at current run rates.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Venkataraman noted that supply chain conditions improved materially throughout the year, enabling lead times to return to pre-pandemic levels in most product categories.
Inventory levels at year-end were $98.3 million, down from $117.6 million at the end of fiscal year 2022, indicating improved inventory turnover.
Days inventory outstanding improved to 42 days from 55 days in the prior year, reflecting the benefits of the company's lean manufacturing initiative launched in early 2022.
Accounts receivable at year-end totaled $134.7 million, with days sales outstanding of 58 days, largely unchanged from the prior year.
The company's long-term debt at the end of fiscal year 2022 stood at $315.0 million, consisting primarily of senior unsecured notes issued in 2019.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano made scheduled principal repayments of $50.0 million on its senior unsecured notes.
Additionally, the company voluntarily prepaid an additional $115.0 million of outstanding principal in the third quarter, taking advantage of its strong cash generation.
As a result of these repayments, the company reduced its outstanding long-term debt balance from $315.0 million to $150.0 million by the end of fiscal year 2023.
The net debt-to-EBITDA ratio improved to 0.7 times at year-end, compared to 1.8 times at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Moody's Investors Service upgraded Veltrano's credit rating to Baa1 from Baa2 in November 2023, citing the company's deleveraging progress and resilient earnings profile.
The upgrade represented the first credit rating improvement for Veltrano in seven years and was welcomed by management as a validation of its balance sheet strategy.
Interest expense for fiscal year 2023 was $18.2 million, down from $24.6 million in the prior year, consistent with the reduction in outstanding debt.
By the close of fiscal year 2023, Veltrano carried no remaining long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having fully retired all outstanding principal through the year's repayments.
Looking ahead, management provided fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for total revenue in the range of $910 million to $940 million, implying growth of approximately 7 to 11 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $215 million to $230 million, reflecting expected operating leverage and continued cost discipline.
The company announced plans to invest approximately $55 million in capital expenditures during fiscal year 2024, with a significant portion allocated to a new R&D center in Austin, Texas.
Headcount is expected to grow by approximately 200 to 250 employees in fiscal year 2024, primarily in engineering and customer success roles.
In January 2024, Veltrano entered into a $200 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of eight financial institutions, replacing a smaller prior facility.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate of SOFR plus 1.35 percent and matures in January 2029.
Management stated that the facility would primarily serve as a liquidity backstop and source of operational flexibility rather than as a vehicle for near-term debt-funded acquisitions.
The audit committee confirmed that KPMG LLP would continue as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
KPMG issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023, with no material weaknesses identified in internal controls.
The proxy statement accompanying the annual report proposed re-election of all eight incumbent directors, including two newly appointed independent directors added in October 2023.

Miriam Okafor concluded her shareholder letter by expressing confidence that Veltrano's disciplined capital allocation, product innovation cadence, and expanding global footprint position the company for sustained value creation.
The annual report on Form 10-K was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 14, 2024, and is available on the company's investor relations website.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is pleased to present its annual financial report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company operates in the medical diagnostics and laboratory services sector, serving hospitals, clinics, and independent healthcare providers across North America.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Ashford opened the report with a message emphasizing the company's commitment to innovation and operational excellence.
Chief Financial Officer Jonathan Pryce noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a transformational period for Veltara Diagnostics.
Total consolidated revenues for 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 12% increase over the prior fiscal year.
The diagnostics segment alone contributed $612 million in revenue, driven by strong demand for oncology and cardiovascular testing panels.
Laboratory services revenue rose to $235 million, up from $198 million in fiscal year 2022.
Gross profit for the year was $391 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 46.2%.
Operating expenses, including research and development, selling, and general and administrative costs, totaled $274 million.
Operating income for the period was $117 million, compared with $94 million in the prior year, reflecting solid cost discipline.
At year-end, Veltara Diagnostics held no outstanding long-term debt on its balance sheet, having retired the final tranche of its senior notes in March 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $58 million, primarily directed toward expanding laboratory automation infrastructure in the Midwest.
The company employed approximately 4,800 full-time staff across its 23 operational sites as of December 31, 2023.
Dr. Ashford highlighted the successful integration of BioTrace Analytics, which was acquired in late 2022 for $130 million.
The BioTrace integration contributed an estimated $42 million in incremental revenue during the fiscal year.
In September 2023, the company finalized a $275 million syndicated credit facility with a consortium of five leading financial institutions.
The new credit facility carries a variable interest rate tied to SOFR plus 150 basis points and matures in 2028.
Net income attributable to common shareholders for 2023 was $81 million, or $2.14 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $63 million, or $1.67 per diluted share, recorded in fiscal year 2022.
The board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Total dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 amounted to $26 million, consistent with the company's capital return policy.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $154 million, representing an EBITDA margin of approximately 18.2%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203 million, up from $162 million at the close of fiscal year 2022.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.4x, reflecting strong short-term liquidity management.
Accounts receivable days outstanding decreased to 47 days from 52 days in the prior year, indicating improved collections performance.
Inventory turnover remained stable at 8.3 times, in line with industry benchmarks for diagnostic reagent management.
The diagnostics segment launched five new testing products during 2023, including the VeltraPlex Respiratory Panel and the ClearPath Metabolic Screen.
The ClearPath Metabolic Screen received FDA 510(k) clearance in June 2023 and was commercially available by August 2023.
Management anticipates that the new product portfolio will contribute between $55 million and $70 million in incremental revenue during fiscal year 2024.
Research and development expenditure for 2023 was $47 million, representing 5.6% of total revenue, up from 4.9% in the prior year.
The company's pipeline includes three additional diagnostic platforms expected to enter clinical validation studies in 2024.
Veltara Diagnostics expanded its geographic footprint by opening two new processing centers in Atlanta, Georgia and Phoenix, Arizona.
The Atlanta facility is expected to serve approximately 300 new client accounts in the Southeast region beginning in early 2024.
The Phoenix center adds roughly 180,000 square feet of laboratory processing capacity, enhancing the company's Western U.S. operations.
Environmental, Social, and Governance initiatives remained a strategic priority, with the company reducing its carbon intensity by 11% year-over-year.
The company sourced 38% of its electricity consumption from renewable energy providers, up from 27% in fiscal year 2022.
Employee engagement scores reached an all-time high of 81%, as reported in the company's annual internal survey conducted in October 2023.
Voluntary employee turnover declined to 9.2%, compared with 12.7% in fiscal year 2022, reflecting improved workforce retention programs.
The company invested $6.3 million in employee training and professional development programs during the fiscal year.
Veltara Diagnostics maintained its ISO 15189 accreditation across all laboratory sites following successful third-party audits conducted in mid-2023.
Risk management disclosures note that reimbursement rate changes from Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services represent a material risk factor.
Management estimates that a 5% reduction in CMS reimbursement rates could reduce annual revenue by approximately $31 million.
The company carries professional liability insurance coverage of $250 million per occurrence, which management considers adequate given current claim trends.
Legal proceedings as of year-end were limited to routine contract disputes, with no material litigation pending against the company.
The company repurchased 1.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 under its existing buyback authorization at an average price of $38.60 per share.
Shares outstanding at December 31, 2023 totaled approximately 37.8 million, reflecting the impact of the repurchase activity.
The audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
The independent auditors issued an unqualified opinion on the company's consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
Management's internal control assessment concluded that Veltara Diagnostics maintained effective internal controls over financial reporting as of December 31, 2023.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 23.4%, slightly above the prior year rate of 22.1% due to changes in state apportionment.
Deferred tax liabilities net of deferred tax assets totaled $18 million as of year-end, primarily related to accelerated depreciation on laboratory equipment.
Looking ahead, management issued guidance for fiscal year 2024 revenues in the range of $920 million to $950 million.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2024 was set between $168 million and $178 million, implying continued margin expansion.
Dr. Ashford closed the report by reaffirming the company's long-term strategy of organic growth supplemented by selective acquisitions in adjacent diagnostic markets.
Veltara Diagnostics remains committed to delivering sustainable value to its shareholders, patients, and the broader healthcare community it serves.
This report contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those projected.
Veltara Diagnostics Corporation is a mid-cap medical technology company headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, specializing in point-of-care testing devices and laboratory automation systems.
The following excerpt is drawn from Veltara's Annual Report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, prepared under United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Sandra Okonkwo expressed measured optimism about the company's performance, citing resilient demand across its core product lines.
Total consolidated revenues for fiscal year 2023 reached $1.84 billion, representing an increase of 11.3 percent compared to the $1.65 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The Diagnostics Equipment segment contributed $1.02 billion to total revenues, while the Reagents and Consumables segment accounted for the remaining $820 million.
Gross profit for the year was $712 million, yielding a gross margin of 38.7 percent, an improvement of approximately 90 basis points over the prior year.
Operating expenses totaled $498 million, inclusive of $134 million in research and development expenditures and $217 million in selling, general, and administrative costs.
Adjusted EBITDA came in at $314 million for fiscal 2023, compared to $271 million in the prior fiscal year, reflecting a 15.9 percent year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to Veltara shareholders was $187 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $3.42, up from $2.91 in the prior year.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Bellefort noted that disciplined cost management and favorable product mix were the primary drivers of margin expansion.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4 percent, slightly below management's initial guidance range of 22 to 24 percent.
Capital expenditures totaled $98 million during the year, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
Free cash flow for fiscal 2023 was $216 million, representing a conversion rate of approximately 115 percent relative to net income.
Veltara ended fiscal 2023 with a cash and cash equivalents balance of $389 million, up from $301 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
The company repurchased 2.1 million shares of common stock during the year under its existing $250 million buyback authorization, at an average price of $61.40 per share.
As of December 31, 2023, approximately $121 million remained available under the share repurchase program.
Dr. Okonkwo highlighted the successful commercial launch of the VeltaPath 900 Series, a next-generation molecular diagnostic platform, as a defining achievement of the fiscal year.
The VeltaPath 900 Series began shipping to customers in the second quarter of 2023 and had been placed in over 1,400 laboratory settings by year-end.
International revenues grew by 17.2 percent year-over-year, now comprising 34 percent of total company revenues, up from 32 percent in the prior year.
Veltara's Asia-Pacific operations were the fastest-growing geographic segment, recording 28 percent revenue growth driven primarily by expanded distribution agreements in Japan and South Korea.
The company added 620 net new full-time employees during fiscal 2023, bringing the total global workforce to approximately 6,880 employees at year-end.
Headcount additions were concentrated in commercial, clinical affairs, and manufacturing functions to support the VeltaPath 900 Series rollout.
Veltara's inventory balance increased to $203 million as of December 31, 2023, compared to $161 million at the prior year-end, reflecting pre-positioning of reagent components.
Days inventory outstanding rose modestly to 74 days, up from 65 days in the comparable prior-year period.
Accounts receivable totaled $298 million at year-end, with days sales outstanding of 59 days, consistent with the prior year's 58-day metric.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.6 times and a quick ratio of 1.9 times, both indicative of a healthy short-term liquidity position.
Veltara's long-term debt as of December 31, 2023 consisted solely of $300 million in senior unsecured notes due 2028, which were issued in 2021.
The senior notes carry a fixed coupon rate of 3.85 percent per annum, with interest paid semi-annually in January and July.
Veltara carried no revolving credit borrowings outstanding as of December 31, 2023, and its balance sheet remained entirely free of any variable-rate credit facility obligations.
The company's net leverage ratio, defined as net debt divided by trailing twelve-month adjusted EBITDA, stood at a modest 0.35 times at fiscal year-end.
Mr. Bellefort emphasized that maintaining a conservative balance sheet is central to management's capital allocation philosophy.
The board of directors approved an increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $0.18 per share, up from $0.15 per share in fiscal 2022, effective with the first quarter 2024 payment.
Total dividends paid during fiscal 2023 amounted to $32.7 million, reflecting the prior dividend rate across all four quarters.
In November 2023, Veltara finalized a $175 million syndicated revolving credit agreement with a consortium of seven institutional lenders, replacing its previous undrawn facility.
The Diagnostics Equipment segment's operating margin expanded by 130 basis points to 22.4 percent, benefiting from manufacturing efficiencies and higher average selling prices.
Reagents and Consumables segment operating margin contracted slightly to 19.1 percent, compared to 19.8 percent in the prior year, due to elevated raw material costs.
Management expects the raw material cost headwinds in the Reagents and Consumables segment to moderate in the first half of fiscal 2024 as supply chain conditions normalize.
Research and development spending of $134 million represented 7.3 percent of total revenues, consistent with the company's stated target range of 7 to 8 percent.
Veltara filed 14 new patent applications during fiscal 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 312 granted patents across 22 jurisdictions.
The company's clinical affairs team completed three pivotal trials during the year, including a landmark study validating the VeltaPath 900 Series for sepsis biomarker detection.
Veltara received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for two new reagent kits targeting cardiac and respiratory panels in the third quarter of 2023.
The company also received CE Mark approval for four assays in the European Union, expanding its addressable market in that region.
For fiscal year 2024, management issued revenue guidance in the range of $2.02 billion to $2.08 billion, implying growth of approximately 10 to 13 percent.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $345 million to $365 million, reflecting anticipated operating leverage as the VeltaPath 900 Series scales.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was provided in the range of $3.75 to $3.95, assuming a weighted average diluted share count of approximately 54.2 million shares.
The company expects capital expenditures in fiscal 2024 to be in the range of $110 million to $125 million, primarily to support a second manufacturing line in Durham.
Dr. Okonkwo stated that Veltara intends to pursue targeted bolt-on acquisitions in the molecular diagnostics and digital health analytics spaces over the next 12 to 18 months.
The company evaluated several potential acquisition targets during fiscal 2023 but did not complete any transactions, as pricing expectations did not meet internal return hurdles.
Veltara's audit committee engaged Ernst & Whitmore LLP as independent auditor, and their report accompanying these financial statements expressed an unqualified opinion.
The company has not identified any material weaknesses in its internal control over financial reporting as assessed under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404(b) framework.
Veltara's stock closed at $68.42 per share on December 29, 2023, the last trading day of the fiscal year, giving the company a market capitalization of approximately $3.73 billion.
The 52-week trading range for Veltara's common stock was $51.17 to $74.88, reflecting periods of both sector-wide pressure and company-specific positive catalysts.

Management believes Veltara is well-positioned to capitalize on the continued secular growth in decentralized diagnostics, which independent research firms project will expand at a compound annual rate of 9 percent through 2028.
The company's diversified customer base, spanning hospital networks, independent clinical laboratories, and government health agencies, provides meaningful revenue stability.

Veltara remains committed to responsible corporate stewardship, having reduced its Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 12 percent relative to the 2020 baseline, consistent with its published sustainability targets.
