The company serves clients in the automotive, aerospace, and consumer goods sectors across 42 countries.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.2% increase over the prior year.
Gross profit margins improved to 38.4%, up from 35.9% in fiscal year 2022, driven largely by favorable raw material pricing.
Chief Financial Officer Daniël Vreeberg attributed the margin expansion to disciplined procurement and supply chain optimization.
Operating expenses totaled €412 million, including €98 million allocated to research and development initiatives.
Veltoran ended fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term borrowings, having retired its final outstanding bond in March of that year.
The polymer additives segment delivered the strongest year-over-year revenue growth at 11.4%, fueled by demand in lightweight vehicle manufacturing.
Industrial coatings revenue grew modestly at 3.1%, constrained by slower-than-expected recovery in commercial construction markets.
Surface treatment solutions posted a 9.8% revenue increase, benefiting from expanded partnerships with two major European aerospace contractors.
Headcount across all global operations stood at 6,840 full-time employees as of December 31, 2023.
In October 2023, Veltoran secured a €350 million syndicated credit facility from a consortium of seven European banks to support its planned acquisition pipeline.
The acquisition pipeline includes three target companies in Eastern Europe and one in Southeast Asia, all in adjacent specialty chemical markets.
Sundqvist noted that the Southeast Asian target would strengthen Veltoran's position in the rapidly growing electronics-grade polymer segment.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to €134 million, focused primarily on capacity expansion at the Antwerp and Gdańsk production facilities.
The Antwerp facility upgrade added approximately 18,000 metric tons of annual coating production capacity.
Depreciation and amortization charges totaled €89 million for the fiscal year, consistent with prior-year levels.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €391 million, yielding an EBITDA margin of 21.3%.
Net income for the year was €198 million, translating to earnings per share of €3.47 on a fully diluted basis.
The board of directors approved a full-year dividend of €1.10 per share, a 10% increase compared to the €1.00 per share paid in fiscal year 2022.
Vreeberg emphasized that the dividend increase reflects confidence in sustainable free cash flow generation.
Free cash flow for the year was €221 million, providing ample coverage for both capital expenditures and shareholder returns.
The company repurchased €45 million of its own shares during the year under its ongoing buyback authorization.
Total shareholder returns, inclusive of dividends and share price appreciation, reached 18.3% for calendar year 2023.
Veltoran's share price closed at €62.40 on December 31, 2023, compared to €53.10 at the end of fiscal year 2022.
The company's market capitalization at year-end was approximately €3.56 billion.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.7%, up from 13.2% the prior year, reflecting more efficient use of deployed assets.
Inventory levels decreased by €31 million year-over-year, reflecting improved demand forecasting and lean manufacturing practices.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 44 days from 49 days in the prior year, indicating stronger collections performance.
Trade payables extended modestly to 62 days, consistent with Veltoran's strategy to optimize working capital without straining supplier relationships.
The company's net working capital position at year-end was €267 million.
During 2023, Veltoran launched two new product lines within the polymer additives segment: VeltaFlex Pro and VeltaShield Ultra.
VeltaFlex Pro targets the flexible packaging market and is projected to reach €40 million in annual revenue by fiscal year 2025.
VeltaShield Ultra is designed for high-temperature industrial applications and received regulatory certification in the European Union in September 2023.
Combined initial customer orders for the two new products exceeded €18 million in the fourth quarter alone.
The research and development pipeline contains 17 active projects at various stages of development, spanning all three business segments.
Veltoran's R&D spending as a percentage of revenue has consistently exceeded the specialty chemicals industry average of 4.1%.
At 5.3% of revenue, the company's R&D intensity reflects its strategic commitment to innovation-led differentiation.
Chief Operating Officer Priya Rathindran highlighted operational efficiency gains achieved through the company's Lean Excellence Program.
The program, launched in 2021, has cumulatively delivered €62 million in cost savings through the end of fiscal year 2023.
Environmental, social, and governance performance remained a strategic priority for the Veltoran board throughout the year.
Carbon emissions intensity per metric ton of product decreased by 6.4% in 2023, contributing to the company's 2030 net-zero roadmap.
Water consumption across manufacturing operations declined by 8.1% compared to the prior year due to closed-loop cooling system upgrades.
Veltoran achieved a Total Recordable Incident Rate of 0.43 per 200,000 hours worked, an improvement from 0.51 in fiscal year 2022.
The company was recognized by the European Chemical Industry Council as a sustainability leader for the second consecutive year.
Looking ahead, management issued fiscal year 2024 guidance calling for revenue growth in the range of 6% to 9%.
EBITDA margin guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at 21.5% to 22.5%, reflecting anticipated benefits from the new Antwerp capacity.
Vreeberg cautioned that margin guidance assumes raw material prices remain within 5% of current levels throughout the year.
Currency headwinds from a strengthening euro relative to the US dollar and Japanese yen are expected to reduce reported revenue by approximately €30 million.
Management also noted geopolitical risks associated with operations in Eastern Europe, which account for roughly 14% of total revenue.
Veltoran's credit rating was reaffirmed at BBB+ by both Fitch and Standard & Poor's following the annual review in November 2023.
The rating agencies cited stable cash flow generation and a conservative financial policy as key credit strengths.
Sundqvist concluded the report by reaffirming Veltoran's commitment to delivering value for all stakeholders through disciplined capital allocation.
The board anticipates presenting a detailed mid-term strategic plan at the investor day scheduled for March 2024.
Veltoran's full audited financial statements, prepared in accordance with IFRS, are available on the company's investor relations website.
The independent audit was conducted by Ernst & Young LLP, which issued an unqualified opinion on the consolidated financial statements.
Veltora Dynamics Corporation is a mid-cap industrial automation and robotics firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio.
The company was founded in 2008 by CEO Margaret Holloway and has grown steadily through a combination of organic expansion and strategic acquisitions.
Veltora Dynamics operates across three primary business segments: precision manufacturing systems, autonomous logistics platforms, and industrial software services.
For the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, the company reported total revenue of $1.42 billion, representing a 9.3% increase over the prior year.
The precision manufacturing systems segment contributed $612 million in revenue, maintaining its position as the largest revenue driver for the organization.
Autonomous logistics platforms generated $498 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand from warehouse and distribution center operators nationwide.
The industrial software services segment posted revenue of $310 million, up 14.7% year-over-year, driven by new enterprise software licensing agreements.
Gross profit for the full year reached $534 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 37.6%, which was an improvement from 35.1% in fiscal 2022.
Operating income came in at $198 million, compared to $171 million in the prior fiscal year, representing a 15.8% improvement.
Chief Financial Officer Daniel Reyes attributed the margin expansion to disciplined cost management and favorable product mix shifts toward higher-margin software offerings.
Net income attributable to common shareholders totaled $143 million, or $2.87 per diluted share, for fiscal year 2023.
This compared favorably to net income of $119 million, or $2.41 per diluted share, reported in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2023 was $267 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 18.8%.
The company invested $89 million in research and development during the year, reflecting its continued commitment to next-generation automation technologies.
Capital expenditures totaled $74 million, primarily directed toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Dayton, Ohio production facility.
Veltora Dynamics ended fiscal 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $231 million, up from $187 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Accounts receivable stood at $196 million, and inventory levels were maintained at $143 million, both within management's targeted ranges.
The company's backlog of confirmed orders reached a record high of $890 million as of December 31, 2023, providing strong visibility into near-term revenue.
Headcount grew to 6,840 full-time employees by year-end, an increase of approximately 420 employees compared to the prior year-end figure.
Veltora Dynamics completed the fiscal year entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final outstanding term loan in March 2023.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.72 per share.
This dividend level represents a 12.5% increase from the $0.64 per share annualized dividend paid during fiscal 2022.
Share repurchases during fiscal 2023 amounted to $45 million, reducing the diluted share count by approximately 1.1 million shares.
The company's return on invested capital improved to 14.2% in fiscal 2023, up from 12.1% in the prior fiscal year.
In October 2023, Veltora Dynamics entered into a $350 million revolving credit agreement with a syndicate of five major commercial banks.
Chief Executive Officer Margaret Holloway stated that the organization remains focused on disciplined capital allocation and sustainable long-term value creation.
The autonomous logistics segment secured three major multi-year contracts totaling $215 million in aggregate contract value during the fiscal year.
These contracts are expected to contribute meaningfully to revenue beginning in the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
Veltora Dynamics launched its next-generation VX-900 robotic picking system in June 2023, receiving strong initial market reception.
The VX-900 platform incorporates advanced machine vision and artificial intelligence capabilities that improve picking accuracy by up to 32% versus prior generations.
By year-end, the company had shipped 1,240 VX-900 units to customers across the North American market.
The industrial software services segment expanded its customer base to 2,170 enterprise clients as of December 31, 2023.
Average annual contract value per enterprise software client increased to $143,000, up from $128,000 in fiscal 2022.
Veltora Dynamics also completed the acquisition of RoboPath Analytics, a small data analytics firm, for $38 million in cash during September 2023.
The RoboPath acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to adjusted EBITDA and to enhance the company's software analytics capabilities.
International revenue accounted for 18% of total revenue in fiscal 2023, with particular strength in Canadian and Mexican markets.
The company entered into a distribution agreement with a European industrial conglomerate to expand its presence in the German and Dutch markets.
Management expects international revenue to represent approximately 24% of total revenue by fiscal year 2025.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses totaled $247 million in fiscal 2023, representing 17.4% of total revenue.
This ratio compares favorably to the 18.9% SG&A-to-revenue ratio recorded in fiscal 2022, reflecting operating leverage across the business.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 22.1%, slightly below management's initial guidance of 23.5%.
This favorable tax outcome was primarily driven by research and development tax credits recognized during the third and fourth quarters.
Veltora Dynamics maintained a current ratio of 2.4 and a quick ratio of 1.8 as of December 31, 2023, indicating a strong liquidity position.
The company's working capital improved to $318 million at year-end, compared to $271 million at the end of fiscal 2022.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Vance noted that supply chain disruptions that had hampered performance in 2022 were largely resolved by mid-2023.
Raw material costs as a percentage of cost of goods sold declined by 180 basis points during fiscal 2023, supporting gross margin improvement.
The precision manufacturing segment introduced two new product lines during the year: the ProForm-7 CNC machining center and the TruAlign laser calibration system.
Both new products received positive reviews from industry analysts and are expected to contribute incrementally to segment revenue in fiscal 2024.
Employee satisfaction scores, as measured by the company's annual internal survey, improved to 78% favorable, compared to 71% favorable in fiscal 2022.
Veltora Dynamics was recognized on the Forbes list of America's Best Mid-Size Employers for the second consecutive year.
The company's environmental, social, and governance initiatives resulted in a 9% reduction in absolute carbon emissions compared to fiscal 2022 levels.
Renewable energy now accounts for 31% of total electricity consumption across all company-owned facilities, up from 22% in the prior year.
Management issued fiscal 2024 guidance calling for revenue in the range of $1.54 billion to $1.58 billion, implying growth of approximately 8% to 11%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $290 million to $305 million, representing margin expansion of roughly 50 to 70 basis points.
Diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2024 was established in the range of $3.10 to $3.25, reflecting anticipated continued profitability improvements.
The board of directors authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2024.
Veltora Dynamics plans to open a new regional service and support center in Dallas, Texas, during the first half of fiscal 2024.
This expansion is intended to support growing demand in the South Central United States and improve response times for existing customers.

Management expressed confidence that the company's strong backlog, expanding software revenues, and operational efficiency initiatives position Veltora Dynamics for continued outperformance in fiscal 2024 and beyond.
Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. is a mid-cap specialty pharmaceutical company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
The company focuses on the development and commercialization of oncology and rare disease therapeutics.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Miriam Castellano presented the annual results to investors during the fourth-quarter earnings call held on February 14, 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Gerald Onslow noted that fiscal year 2023 represented a pivotal inflection point for Veltrano's commercial operations.
Total net revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847 million, representing a 22% increase over the prior year.
The company's flagship oncology product, Nexavril, generated $512 million in net sales during the fiscal year.
Nexavril's revenue growth was driven primarily by expanded formulary access across three major commercial insurers in the United States.
The rare disease segment, anchored by Corathex, contributed $218 million to total net revenue.
Corathex received supplemental approval from the FDA in March 2023 for a second rare pediatric indication.
The remaining $117 million in net revenue came from contract manufacturing services and licensing royalties.
Gross profit for the fiscal year stood at $631 million, reflecting a gross margin of approximately 74.5%.
Research and development expenditures totaled $194 million, equal to roughly 23% of net revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses reached $187 million, slightly below management's internal guidance of $195 million.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was reported at $250 million, compared with $163 million in fiscal year 2022.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $198 million, or $2.84 per diluted share.
Veltrano ended fiscal year 2023 with cash and cash equivalents of $403 million on its balance sheet.
The company generated $271 million in operating cash flow during the year, a 31% improvement year-over-year.
Capital expenditures amounted to $58 million, primarily related to the expansion of the company's Durham manufacturing facility.
Free cash flow for the fiscal year therefore stood at $213 million, providing ample flexibility for strategic deployment.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $150 million, effective January 1, 2024.
Dr. Castellano emphasized that the company's pipeline remains the primary driver of long-term shareholder value creation.
Veltrano's pipeline currently includes seven clinical-stage compounds across oncology, immunology, and rare metabolic disorders.
The most advanced pipeline asset, VT-4418, is a first-in-class KRAS G12C inhibitor currently in Phase 3 clinical trials.
Enrollment for the Phase 3 study of VT-4418 was completed in October 2023, with topline data expected in the second half of 2024.
A second late-stage asset, VT-3291, targeting refractory multiple myeloma, recently advanced into a pivotal Phase 2/3 combination study.
Head of Research Dr. Leonora Pfiester noted that preclinical data for two additional compounds exceeded internal efficacy benchmarks.
Veltrano's employee headcount grew to 2,340 full-time equivalents by December 31, 2023, up from 1,980 at the end of 2022.
The majority of new hires were concentrated in commercial operations and medical affairs, reflecting the company's growth-stage priorities.
International expansion remained a strategic focus, with regulatory submissions filed in the European Union and Japan for Nexavril.
The European Medicines Agency accepted the Marketing Authorisation Application for Nexavril in November 2023.
Management expects European approval, if granted, to contribute incremental revenue beginning in the second half of 2025.
Veltrano's partnership with Kyomura Pharmaceutical in Japan is expected to generate milestone payments of up to $75 million.
Mr. Onslow described the company's balance sheet as conservatively managed, with a focus on preserving financial flexibility.
Long-term debt on the balance sheet as of December 31, 2023, consisted solely of a $50 million term loan entered in 2019.
Veltrano carried no outstanding borrowings under any revolving credit facility at the close of fiscal year 2023.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 48 days, down from 57 days in the prior fiscal year, reflecting tighter collection processes.
Inventory levels at year-end were $62 million, consistent with the company's policy of maintaining three to four months of product supply.
The company recorded a $14 million non-cash impairment charge related to a discontinued dermatology asset acquired in 2021.
Excluding this impairment, adjusted operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $264 million.
In January 2024, Veltrano drew down $180 million against a newly established syndicated revolving credit facility to fund near-term pipeline investments.
The tax provision for fiscal year 2023 was $52 million, reflecting an effective tax rate of approximately 20.8%.
Deferred tax assets of $38 million relate primarily to net operating loss carryforwards from legacy acquisitions.
The company's audit committee confirmed that PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP issued an unqualified opinion on the fiscal year 2023 financial statements.
Veltrano declared and paid a special cash dividend of $0.50 per share in December 2023, totaling approximately $35 million in aggregate.
Management stated that ordinary recurring dividends are not part of the current capital allocation strategy, given ongoing pipeline investment needs.
The company's total stockholders' equity at year-end was $1.12 billion, up from $0.94 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Goodwill and intangible assets collectively represent $780 million on the balance sheet, predominantly from the 2020 acquisition of Ardenvax Therapeutics.
Ardenvax contributed the Corathex intellectual property and associated rare disease commercial infrastructure.
Dr. Castellano confirmed that integration of Ardenvax is fully complete and that no further restructuring charges are anticipated.
Veltrano's market capitalization as of December 31, 2023, was approximately $6.1 billion, based on a closing share price of $87.62.
The company's stock outperformed the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by approximately 18 percentage points over the calendar year 2023.
Institutional ownership stands at approximately 78% of shares outstanding, with top holders including Vanguard, BlackRock, and Fidelity.
Mr. Onslow provided fiscal year 2024 guidance of net revenue in the range of $940 million to $975 million.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $3.20 to $3.45.
The guidance range assumes no contribution from potential European Nexavril revenues and no one-time milestone receipts.
Veltrano's 2024 R&D budget is expected to increase to approximately $225 million to $240 million, reflecting Phase 3 execution costs for VT-4418.
The company also plans to initiate a Phase 1 study for VT-5502, a novel antibody-drug conjugate targeting solid tumors, in the first quarter of 2024.
Dr. Pfiester expressed confidence that the company's translational medicine capabilities distinguish Veltrano from peers in the mid-cap biotech space.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives were highlighted in the annual report, including a commitment to carbon neutrality by 2030.
Veltrano donated $4.2 million to patient advocacy organizations and access programs in 2023, an increase from $3.1 million in 2022.
The board of directors currently comprises nine members, five of whom are independent, satisfying NASDAQ listing requirements.
Management expressed confidence that Veltrano's diversified commercial portfolio, robust pipeline, and strengthened balance sheet position the company for sustained growth through 2026 and beyond.
This excerpt is derived from the Veltrano Biosciences, Inc. Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 28, 2024.
Veltoran Industries, a mid-cap specialty chemicals manufacturer headquartered in Rotterdam, Netherlands, released its full-year financial results for fiscal year 2023 on March 14, 2024.
Chief Executive Officer Marieke van den Broeck opened the earnings call by noting that the company had navigated a challenging macroeconomic environment with considerable resilience.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached €1.84 billion, representing a 7.3% increase compared to €1.71 billion reported in fiscal year 2022.
The company's core specialty coatings segment contributed €920 million, or approximately 50% of consolidated revenues, up from €840 million in the prior year.
Veltoran's adhesives and sealants division posted revenues of €514 million, growing 9.1% year-over-year driven by strong demand from the European automotive sector.
The performance materials segment, which includes advanced polymer solutions, generated €406 million in revenue, modestly below management's initial target of €430 million.
Chief Financial Officer Hendrik Strausberg attributed the shortfall in performance materials to supply chain disruptions in the first half of the year that affected raw material availability.
Gross profit for the full year was €607 million, yielding a gross margin of 33.0%, compared to a gross margin of 31.4% in the prior fiscal year.
Operating income rose to €221 million, translating to an operating margin of 12.0%, up from 10.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at €298 million, reflecting an EBITDA margin of 16.2%.
Veltoran Industries closed fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired the last tranche of its 2019 syndicated loan in October 2023.
Net income attributable to shareholders was €154 million, corresponding to earnings per share of €3.22, compared to €2.81 in the prior year.
The Board of Directors approved a full-year dividend of €1.10 per share, a 10% increase over the €1.00 per share paid in fiscal year 2022.
Capital expenditure totaled €138 million in fiscal year 2023, focused primarily on expanding the Rotterdam main campus and upgrading automated production lines.
The company ended the fiscal year with a cash and cash equivalents balance of €312 million, down from €347 million at the start of the year.
In November 2023, Veltoran secured a €250 million revolving credit facility with a consortium of five European banks to support strategic expansion initiatives.
Head count across all global operations stood at 8,740 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, a net addition of 310 employees relative to the prior year-end.
Research and development expenditure increased to €92 million, representing 5.0% of revenues, as the company accelerated innovation in bio-based coating technologies.
Veltoran filed 34 new patent applications in 2023, bringing its active patent portfolio to 412 patents globally, a record for the company.
The company's North American operations, managed from a regional hub in Charlotte, North Carolina, contributed revenues of €370 million, up 12% year-over-year.
Asia-Pacific revenues reached €284 million, with particularly strong growth in South Korea and Vietnam where new distribution agreements were signed in mid-2023.
European revenues, which remain the company's largest geographic segment, totaled €1.19 billion, representing 64.6% of global consolidated sales.
Van den Broeck emphasized that Veltoran's sustainability roadmap had advanced significantly, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions reduced by 11% year-on-year.
The company achieved its ISO 14001 recertification across all five manufacturing sites, reinforcing its commitment to environmental management best practices.
Veltoran also published its inaugural Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures report, aligning its climate risk disclosures with international investor expectations.
Strausberg highlighted that working capital management improved materially, with days sales outstanding declining from 58 days to 51 days during fiscal year 2023.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.2 times annually, reflecting tighter demand forecasting and the rollout of a new enterprise resource planning system in Q2 2023.
The new ERP platform, implemented in partnership with a leading enterprise software provider, is expected to generate annualized efficiency savings of approximately €18 million by 2025.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were €247 million, representing 13.4% of revenues, compared to 14.1% in the prior fiscal year.
The decline in SG&A as a percentage of revenues reflects both cost discipline initiatives and improved leverage from higher top-line volumes.
Veltoran completed the acquisition of Belgian specialty resins firm Brockhaven NV in August 2023 for a total consideration of €195 million.
The Brockhaven integration is progressing ahead of schedule, with synergy realization expected to reach €22 million annually within 36 months of closing.
Strausberg noted that the acquisition was funded entirely through the company's existing cash reserves, without any recourse to external financing instruments.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.4%, slightly above the 21.8% rate recorded in fiscal year 2022 due to changes in the Dutch corporate tax regime.
Deferred tax liabilities increased by €14 million during the year, primarily associated with accelerated depreciation allowances on newly commissioned plant equipment.
Return on invested capital improved to 14.3%, compared to 12.7% in fiscal year 2022, as the company extracted greater value from its asset base.
The company's net promoter score among key industrial customers reached 62 in an independent survey conducted in Q4 2023, the highest level ever recorded by Veltoran.
Customer concentration risk remains moderate, with the top ten customers accounting for approximately 28% of total revenues, consistent with the prior year.
Veltoran launched three new product lines under its EcoShield brand in September 2023, targeting low-VOC applications in the construction and marine coating sectors.
Early commercial traction for EcoShield has been promising, with orders received through December 31, 2023 already totaling €47 million in anticipated forward revenue.
The company entered into a joint development agreement with a major German automotive OEM in Q3 2023 to co-develop next-generation surface treatment chemistries.
This partnership is anticipated to yield commercial products ready for serial production by late 2026, pending successful completion of qualification testing.
Veltoran's defined benefit pension obligations stood at €183 million at year-end, partially offset by plan assets valued at €141 million.
The pension funding gap narrowed by €9 million during fiscal year 2023, reflecting strong investment performance and an additional discretionary employer contribution of €6 million.
Moody's reaffirmed Veltoran's Baa2 issuer credit rating in December 2023, citing the company's stable earnings profile and diversified product portfolio.
S&P Global also maintained its BBB stable outlook on Veltoran, acknowledging the company's improved operating margins and solid liquidity position.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for revenue in the range of €1.92 billion to €1.98 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 5% to 8%.
EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at €315 million to €330 million, reflecting ongoing margin expansion from operational efficiency programs.
Van den Broeck cautioned that geopolitical uncertainty in Eastern Europe and fluctuating raw material costs for titanium dioxide and epoxy resins remain key watch items.
The company plans to increase capital expenditure to approximately €160 million in fiscal year 2024, with the largest allocations directed toward sustainability infrastructure.
Veltoran's share price closed at €41.50 on March 13, 2024, the day prior to the results announcement, valuing the company at approximately €1.99 billion.
Following the earnings release, shares rose 4.2% in early Amsterdam trading, reflecting positive investor reception of the full-year results and 2024 guidance.
Van den Broeck concluded by reiterating Veltoran's long-term ambition to achieve €2.5 billion in revenues and a 20% EBITDA margin by fiscal year 2028.
The full annual report and audited financial statements prepared by PricewaterhouseCoopers are available on Veltoran's investor relations website as of today's date.
Veltrano Systems Corporation is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure provider headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by Chief Executive Officer Damien Holt and Chief Technology Officer Priya Sundaram.
Veltrano specializes in delivering integrated supply chain optimization platforms to manufacturing and logistics clients across North America and Europe.
The fiscal year ending December 31, 2023 marked a significant milestone in the company's operational history.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase compared to the prior year.
Subscription-based recurring revenue accounted for 68% of total revenue, up from 61% in fiscal year 2022.
The company's gross profit margin improved to 61.4%, driven by increased software license attachment rates and reduced hosting costs.
Operating expenses totaled $412.6 million for the full year, inclusive of research and development, sales, and general administrative costs.
Research and development expenditures reached $138.9 million, equivalent to approximately 16.4% of total revenue.
CEO Damien Holt noted in his annual letter to shareholders that innovation remained the company's foremost strategic priority.
Veltrano's headcount grew from 4,210 employees at the start of fiscal year 2023 to 4,875 employees by year-end.
The company expanded its European operations significantly, opening new offices in Amsterdam, Warsaw, and Lyon during the second half of 2023.
Veltrano completed the acquisition of Nexlara Analytics, a Munich-based data integration firm, in March 2023 for €94 million.
The Nexlara acquisition added approximately 210 employees and introduced a suite of predictive analytics tools to Veltrano's portfolio.
Integration of the Nexlara platform was completed ahead of schedule in October 2023, roughly three months earlier than projected.
Chief Financial Officer Renata Osei reported that operating income for the fiscal year totaled $107.4 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $193.7 million, reflecting a margin of approximately 22.9%.
Net income attributable to common shareholders reached $72.1 million, compared to $48.6 million in fiscal year 2022.
Diluted earnings per share for fiscal year 2023 were $1.84, based on approximately 39.2 million weighted average diluted shares outstanding.
Veltrano exited fiscal year 2023 carrying no long-term debt obligations on its balance sheet, having repaid the final tranche of its prior credit facility in February 2023.
Capital expenditures for the year amounted to $44.2 million, focused primarily on data center capacity expansion and internal tooling.
The company generated $161.8 million in operating cash flow, representing a strong conversion ratio relative to net income.
Free cash flow for fiscal year 2023 stood at $117.6 million after deducting capital expenditures from operating cash flow.
Veltrano's cash and cash equivalents balance at December 31, 2023 stood at $289.4 million.
In November 2023, Veltrano secured a $350 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of five institutional lenders to support strategic growth initiatives.
The board of directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million effective January 2024.
During fiscal year 2023, Veltrano repurchased approximately 1.1 million shares at an average price of $38.60 per share.
Total shareholder equity at year-end stood at $534.7 million, reflecting accumulated retained earnings and the impact of share repurchases.
The company did not declare or pay any cash dividends during fiscal year 2023, consistent with its policy of reinvesting earnings into growth.
Veltrano's North American segment generated revenue of $578.2 million, representing 68.3% of total consolidated revenue.
The European segment contributed $221.8 million in revenue, with year-over-year growth of 31.4% partly attributable to the Nexlara acquisition.
The Asia-Pacific segment, launched in late 2022, generated $47.3 million in revenue during its first full fiscal year of operations.
Veltrano added 312 new enterprise customers in fiscal year 2023, bringing the total active customer count to 2,847.
Net revenue retention rate for the year was 118%, indicating strong expansion within the existing customer base.
The company's flagship product, VeltraCore 5.0, was released in April 2023 following an eighteen-month development cycle.
VeltraCore 5.0 introduced machine learning-driven demand forecasting capabilities that reduced customer inventory error rates by an average of 23%.
By year-end, approximately 61% of Veltrano's active customers had migrated to VeltraCore 5.0 from prior versions.
Customer satisfaction scores as measured by the company's annual Net Promoter Survey reached 72, up from 65 in the prior year.
Chief Revenue Officer Marcus Telles attributed the strong NPS improvement to enhancements in customer onboarding workflows and support response times.
Average support ticket resolution time declined from 11.4 hours in 2022 to 7.9 hours in 2023, a 31% improvement.
Veltrano's partner ecosystem expanded to include 148 certified implementation partners globally, up from 103 at the end of 2022.
The company invested $22.7 million in partner enablement programs, certification infrastructure, and co-marketing initiatives during fiscal year 2023.
Marketing and sales expenses totaled $187.4 million for the year, representing 22.1% of total revenue.
Veltrano maintained a sales efficiency ratio of 0.74, meaning the company generated $0.74 of new annual recurring revenue for every $1.00 spent on sales and marketing.
The company's backlog of contracted but unrecognized revenue stood at $403.6 million as of December 31, 2023.
Weighted average remaining contract duration across the customer base was 2.8 years.
Deferred revenue on the balance sheet increased by $38.2 million year-over-year, reaching $174.5 million at fiscal year-end.
CFO Renata Osei emphasized that the deferred revenue growth was a leading indicator of future recognized revenue strength.
Veltrano's inventory of hardware appliances used in certain on-premise deployments decreased to $11.3 million from $18.7 million the prior year.
The decline in hardware inventory reflected the company's strategic shift toward cloud-native deployments, which reduced reliance on physical appliances.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved to 47 days from 54 days in fiscal year 2022, reflecting tightened collection processes.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, compared to 23.1% in the prior year.
The reduction in effective tax rate was primarily attributable to research and development tax credits recognized in the third quarter.
Veltrano accrued $14.8 million in restructuring charges related to workforce optimization following the Nexlara integration.
The restructuring primarily affected overlapping support and back-office functions, with approximately 95 positions eliminated.
Despite the restructuring, total net headcount still increased year-over-year due to hiring in product development and sales.
Looking ahead to fiscal year 2024, management issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $960 million to $985 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 is projected to fall between $215 million and $230 million.
The guidance reflects anticipated continued momentum in the European segment and the initial contribution from the Asia-Pacific expansion strategy.
Management acknowledged that macroeconomic uncertainty and potential fluctuations in foreign exchange rates represent key risks to achieving the guidance.
Veltrano's independent auditor, Kellerman & Voss LLP, issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements for fiscal year 2023.
The audit committee confirmed that no material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting were identified during the fiscal year.
CTO Priya Sundaram announced in January 2024 that the company would invest an incremental $30 million in artificial intelligence research over the coming two fiscal years.
The board of directors expressed confidence in Veltrano's long-term strategic direction and reaffirmed commitment to sustainable, profitable growth through fiscal year 2026.
This report contains forward-looking statements subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those anticipated.

Harvestline Agritech Corporation, a leading precision agriculture technology firm headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, released its consolidated annual report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Margaret Olusegun and CFO Patrick Drennan, both of whom continue to lead the organization today.
Harvestline operates across three primary segments: Smart Sensors, Data Analytics, and Field Robotics.
Total consolidated revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $847.3 million, representing a 14.2% increase over the prior year.
The Smart Sensors segment contributed $312.6 million to total revenue, up 9.8% year-over-year.
The Data Analytics segment generated $298.4 million in revenue, reflecting strong demand for predictive crop yield software.
The Field Robotics segment reported $236.3 million in revenue, driven by robust adoption of autonomous harvesting units.
Gross profit for the year totaled $421.7 million, yielding an overall gross margin of approximately 49.8%.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, amounted to $178.5 million for the full year.
Research and development expenditures reached $89.2 million, representing 10.5% of total revenue.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses came to $89.3 million, consistent with the prior year as a percentage of revenue.
Operating income for fiscal 2023 was $243.2 million, compared with $198.4 million in fiscal 2022.
Net income attributable to Harvestline shareholders was $187.6 million, or $3.42 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $151.8 million, or $2.78 per diluted share, recorded in fiscal 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $291.4 million, reflecting an EBITDA margin of 34.4%.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end stood at $203.1 million, up from $167.5 million at the close of fiscal 2022.
Total assets on the consolidated balance sheet were $1.42 billion as of December 31, 2023.
The company's current ratio improved to 2.7x, compared with 2.3x at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Accounts receivable totaled $134.2 million, with days sales outstanding of approximately 58 days.
Inventory on hand at year-end was $76.4 million, a modest increase from $71.1 million in the prior period.
Capital expenditures for fiscal 2023 were $52.3 million, directed primarily toward manufacturing capacity expansion.
Free cash flow for the year amounted to $192.8 million, a 21.3% increase relative to fiscal 2022.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2024.
This dividend level represents a 12.5% increase over the quarterly distribution declared in the prior year.
Harvestline repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares during fiscal 2023 at an average price of $61.40 per share.
Total share repurchase expenditure for the year was approximately $73.7 million under the company's authorized buyback program.
The buyback program had a remaining authorization of $126.3 million as of December 31, 2023.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Mbeki noted that supply chain resilience was a key strategic priority throughout the year.
The company successfully diversified its component sourcing base, reducing reliance on any single supplier to below 15% of total procurement.
Harvestline entered into a strategic partnership with Verdant Genomics in Q2 2023 to co-develop drought-resistant crop monitoring algorithms.
The partnership is expected to yield commercially deployable products by mid-2025, according to the company's development timeline.
During the year, Harvestline expanded its international footprint, establishing new distribution agreements in Brazil, Australia, and the Netherlands.
International revenue grew to $189.6 million in fiscal 2023, representing 22.4% of total revenue.
The company's North American revenue remained its largest regional contributor at $657.7 million.
Harvestline employed 3,847 full-time equivalent staff globally as of December 31, 2023, a net increase of 214 employees compared to year-end 2022.
Employee retention rates improved to 91.3% in fiscal 2023, up from 88.6% in the prior year.
The company invested $14.7 million in employee training and development programs during the year.
Margaret Olusegun commented that talent acquisition in data science and robotics engineering remained highly competitive across the industry.
Harvestline launched its next-generation HarvestOS 4.0 platform in September 2023, combining machine learning with real-time field telemetry.
HarvestOS 4.0 achieved over 2,400 enterprise client activations within its first three months of availability.
Customer satisfaction scores for HarvestOS 4.0 averaged 4.6 out of 5.0 based on post-deployment surveys.
The Field Robotics segment delivered its one-thousandth autonomous harvester unit to a large-scale wheat producer in Kansas in November 2023.
Warranty and service contract revenues within Field Robotics grew 18.7% year-over-year, indicating strong post-sale engagement.
Patrick Drennan highlighted that the company's balance sheet remained exceptionally clean entering fiscal 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Harvestline carried no outstanding long-term debt obligations on its consolidated balance sheet.
The company's credit ratings from both Moody's and S&P were reaffirmed at investment-grade levels during the fourth quarter.
Risk factors disclosed in the annual filing include potential commodity price volatility and evolving data privacy regulations.
Harvestline's effective tax rate for fiscal 2023 was 21.4%, benefiting from R&D tax credits applicable in multiple jurisdictions.
Deferred tax assets totaled $28.3 million, largely related to timing differences in depreciation and stock-based compensation.
The company's pension obligations, covering legacy defined-benefit plans, totaled $41.2 million and were 94.7% funded as of year-end.
In January 2024, Harvestline completed the drawdown of a $250 million revolving credit facility arranged with a syndicate of four commercial banks.
The credit facility carries a variable interest rate based on SOFR plus a margin of 125 basis points and matures in January 2029.
Proceeds from the facility are intended to support Harvestline's planned acquisition of PrecisionRoot Analytics, announced in February 2024.
The PrecisionRoot acquisition is valued at approximately $310 million, inclusive of assumed liabilities, representing a 9.2x forward EBITDA multiple.
Management expects the acquisition to be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 18 months of closing.
Harvestline's backlog of confirmed customer orders as of December 31, 2023, stood at $534.7 million, up 19.6% from year-end 2022.
The backlog provides significant revenue visibility heading into fiscal 2024, management noted in its outlook commentary.
For fiscal year 2024, Harvestline issued guidance for total revenue in the range of $980 million to $1.01 billion.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal 2024 was set at $335 million to $355 million, implying continued margin expansion.
The company anticipates capital expenditures of between $60 million and $70 million in fiscal 2024 to support growth initiatives.
Harvestline's Board of Directors consists of nine members, five of whom are independent directors meeting NASDAQ governance standards.
The Audit Committee, chaired by director Eloise Tambara, oversees internal controls and financial reporting integrity.
Management expressed confidence that Harvestline is well-positioned to capitalize on the global shift toward data-driven, sustainable agriculture.
The full financial statements, notes, and auditor's report are included in the company's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
Meridian Biopharma Holdings, Inc. reported its full-year financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023, reflecting a period of significant operational transformation.
The company, headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, operates across three primary segments: therapeutic drug development, diagnostics manufacturing, and specialty distribution.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Vivienne Hartwell opened the annual review by emphasizing the firm's commitment to long-term shareholder value creation.
Meridian Biopharma recorded total consolidated revenues of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The diagnostics manufacturing segment contributed the largest share of revenue, accounting for approximately 47% of total consolidated sales.
Meridian Biopharma entered fiscal year 2023 entirely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in December 2022.
Gross profit margin improved to 58.4% from 55.1% in the prior year, driven by favorable product mix and manufacturing efficiencies.
Chief Financial Officer Mr. Raymond Okello attributed the margin expansion to disciplined procurement practices and reduced raw material costs.
Research and development expenditures totaled $312 million, representing 17% of total revenues and reflecting the company's pipeline investment strategy.
The therapeutic drug development segment advanced four late-stage clinical candidates, with two submissions expected before the end of the first quarter of 2024.
During the third quarter of 2023, Meridian Biopharma secured a $450 million syndicated term loan facility from a consortium of institutional lenders.
Operating income for the full year reached $274 million, a 19.6% increase relative to fiscal year 2022.
The company generated free cash flow of $198 million, underscoring strong operational execution despite elevated capital expenditure requirements.
Capital expenditures totaled $143 million, primarily directed toward the expansion of the Durham biomanufacturing campus.
Headcount at year-end stood at 8,742 full-time equivalent employees, an increase of 614 employees compared to December 31, 2022.
The specialty distribution segment experienced softer demand in the second half of the year, partly attributed to inventory destocking by hospital networks.
Nevertheless, the specialty distribution segment maintained positive operating margins throughout all four quarters of fiscal year 2023.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $389 million, representing 21.1% of revenues, marginally above the prior year ratio of 20.8%.
Dr. Hartwell noted that investments in commercial infrastructure were deliberate and aligned with the anticipated launch of two flagship therapeutic products.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $183 million, corresponding to diluted earnings per share of $2.71.
This represents an improvement from fiscal year 2022 net income of $149 million and diluted earnings per share of $2.21.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 22.8%, consistent with prior guidance of approximately 22% to 24%.
Meridian Biopharma repurchased 3.2 million shares of common stock during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $41.60 per share.
Total share repurchases amounted to approximately $133 million, funded through operating cash flows.
The Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, representing a 12.5% increase from the previous quarterly dividend of $0.16.
Cumulative dividends paid during fiscal year 2023 totaled $48.6 million.
Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $521 million, providing the company with substantial liquidity heading into fiscal year 2024.
Short-term investments totaling $87 million in U.S. Treasury securities supplemented the company's overall liquidity position.
Accounts receivable days outstanding improved modestly to 47 days from 51 days in the prior year, reflecting enhanced collections processes.
Inventory levels increased to $224 million from $196 million, as the company built strategic buffer stock ahead of new product launches.
The diagnostics manufacturing segment launched three new point-of-care assay products during fiscal year 2023, expanding its test menu to 64 distinct analytes.
These new assay launches were supported by regulatory approvals received in the first and second quarters of the year.
International revenues represented 34% of total consolidated revenues, with growth particularly strong in the European Union and Southeast Asian markets.
The company's European operations generated $312 million in revenue, a 21% increase year-over-year driven by expanded distribution agreements.
Mr. Okello highlighted that the favorable euro-to-dollar exchange rate environment contributed approximately $18 million to European revenue on a reported basis.
Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2023 was $398 million, yielding an adjusted EBITDA margin of 21.6%.
This compares favorably to adjusted EBITDA of $331 million and a margin of 20.3% in fiscal year 2022.
The company incurred $22 million in restructuring charges during fiscal year 2023, primarily related to the consolidation of two legacy distribution centers.
These restructuring activities are expected to generate annual cost savings of approximately $14 million beginning in fiscal year 2024.
Meridian Biopharma also recognized a $9 million impairment charge on an intangible asset associated with a discontinued product line.
The impairment had no cash impact and was excluded from adjusted non-GAAP financial measures.
Total assets at December 31, 2023, were $3.12 billion, reflecting growth from $2.89 billion at the end of fiscal year 2022.
Goodwill and other intangible assets collectively represented $1.14 billion of total assets, stemming largely from the 2019 acquisition of Centurion Diagnostics.
Shareholders' equity increased to $1.67 billion from $1.54 billion at the end of the prior year.
The company's return on equity for fiscal year 2023 was 11.3%, compared to 9.9% in fiscal year 2022.
Dr. Hartwell expressed confidence in the company's pipeline, highlighting the anticipated FDA approval decision for MBH-4412, a novel autoimmune therapy.
MBH-4412 demonstrated statistically significant efficacy in its Phase 3 trial, with a p-value of 0.003 and a favorable safety profile.
The company has pre-submitted its New Drug Application and expects formal FDA acceptance within the first quarter of 2024.
Revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 was established in the range of $2.00 billion to $2.08 billion, implying organic growth of approximately 8.7% to 13.0%.
Adjusted diluted earnings per share guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set between $3.05 and $3.25, reflecting expected leverage on the revenue growth.
The company indicated that capital expenditures for fiscal year 2024 are expected to be in the range of $155 million to $175 million.
Increased capital spending will primarily support the construction of a new fill-finish facility at the Durham campus.
Mr. Okello confirmed that the company has adequate liquidity to fund both its capital program and potential opportunistic acquisitions.
Meridian Biopharma's credit ratings were reaffirmed by two major rating agencies during the fourth quarter of 2023.
Moody's maintained a Baa2 issuer rating with a stable outlook, and S&P Global affirmed a BBB rating with a stable outlook.
The company entered into two separate licensing agreements during fiscal year 2023, expanding its intellectual property portfolio in oncology diagnostics.
Combined upfront licensing fees of $27 million were recognized as other income during the year.
The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors confirmed the engagement of Deloitte & Touche LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal year 2024.
Deloitte issued an unqualified audit opinion on the fiscal year 2023 consolidated financial statements.
The company did not identify any material weaknesses in internal controls over financial reporting during fiscal year 2023.
Meridian Biopharma's annual report on Form 10-K will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before March 15, 2024.
Dr. Hartwell closed her remarks by reaffirming the company's strategic pillars: innovation, operational excellence, and disciplined capital allocation.
The Board and management team collectively expressed strong conviction in Meridian Biopharma's positioning for sustainable growth through the remainder of the decade.
Investor relations inquiries may be directed to Ms. Clara Jennings, Vice President of Investor Relations, at the company's Raleigh headquarters.
The foregoing financial data is presented in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles unless otherwise noted as non-GAAP measures.
Veltora Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software and cloud infrastructure company headquartered in Austin, Texas.
The company was founded in 2009 by CEO Marcus Delray and CTO Priya Nandakumar, both veterans of the enterprise technology sector.
Veltora Systems reported full-year revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 12.3% increase over the prior year.
The company serves over 4,200 enterprise clients across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.
Chief Financial Officer Helena Brosz presented the annual results to investors during a conference call held on February 15, 2024.
Subscription-based recurring revenue now accounts for 71% of total revenue, up from 63% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 reached $312 million, compared to $267 million in the previous year.
Veltora's cloud infrastructure segment, branded as VeltCloud, posted segment revenue of $740 million, a 19% year-over-year increase.
The enterprise software licensing segment contributed $620 million in revenue, while professional services added $480 million.
Gross margin improved by 180 basis points to reach 67.4%, driven primarily by efficiency gains in the cloud segment.
Veltora Systems entered fiscal year 2023 completely free of long-term debt obligations, having retired its final bond tranche in December 2022.
The company's headcount grew to 9,800 full-time employees by year-end, up from 8,950 at the start of 2023.
Capital expenditures totaled $198 million during fiscal year 2023, largely directed toward data center expansion.
The board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of March 1, 2024.
Research and development spending reached $284 million, representing 15.4% of total revenue for the fiscal year.
During the third quarter of fiscal year 2023, Veltora secured a $350 million revolving credit facility from a consortium of six lending institutions.
The company's cash and cash equivalents stood at $512 million as of December 31, 2023.
Veltora's net income for fiscal year 2023 was $224 million, yielding a net profit margin of approximately 12.2%.
Earnings per diluted share came in at $3.41, beating the analyst consensus estimate of $3.28 by $0.13.
The company repurchased approximately 2.1 million shares during fiscal year 2023 at an average price of $57.40 per share.
Marcus Delray highlighted the strong performance of VeltCloud as a primary growth driver in his letter to shareholders.
Veltora's international revenue accounted for 38% of total revenue, with EMEA representing the fastest-growing geographic segment.
Sales and marketing expenses totaled $321 million, reflecting ongoing investment in brand expansion and channel partnerships.
The company launched three new product modules under its VeltAnalytics suite during the second half of fiscal year 2023.
Customer churn rate for the subscription business declined to 4.2%, down from 5.1% in the prior fiscal year.
Priya Nandakumar indicated that the company's artificial intelligence integration roadmap is on track for a phased rollout beginning in mid-2024.
Veltora's accounts receivable days outstanding improved from 51 days to 46 days during fiscal year 2023.
The company maintained an inventory balance of $34 million, primarily consisting of hardware components used in on-premise deployments.
Deferred revenue grew by 22% year-over-year to reach $418 million, signaling strong forward bookings.
Veltora Systems did not complete any acquisitions during fiscal year 2023, focusing instead on organic growth initiatives.
Helena Brosz confirmed that the company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.3%, consistent with the prior year.
The weighted average cost of capital was estimated at 8.7%, which management used as the primary hurdle rate for capital allocation decisions.
Veltora's stock price appreciated by 34% over the course of fiscal year 2023, outperforming the broader technology sector index.
The audit committee reviewed and approved all financial statements in compliance with GAAP and Sarbanes-Oxley requirements.
Operating cash flow for fiscal year 2023 was $398 million, providing ample liquidity to support ongoing operations and strategic initiatives.
The company's debt-to-equity ratio as reported on the December 31, 2023 balance sheet was 0.41, reflecting the new credit facility drawn during the year.
Veltora's top ten clients collectively represented 28% of total revenue, indicating a moderately diversified customer base.
The company increased its annual revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024 to a range of $2.05 billion to $2.12 billion.
Helena Brosz noted that foreign currency headwinds reduced reported revenue by approximately $47 million compared to constant-currency results.
The board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $300 million over the next 24 months, effective January 1, 2024.
Veltora's North American segment posted revenue of $1.14 billion, maintaining its position as the company's largest regional market.
The company recognized a one-time restructuring charge of $18 million in the fourth quarter related to the consolidation of two regional offices.
VeltCloud's annualized recurring revenue crossed the $800 million threshold in December 2023, a milestone management celebrated publicly.
Total assets on the balance sheet increased to $3.2 billion as of December 31, 2023, up from $2.87 billion at the prior year-end.
Veltora's pension obligations declined slightly during fiscal year 2023 due to favorable actuarial assumptions and strong plan asset returns.
The company maintained a current ratio of 2.3, reflecting solid short-term liquidity relative to current liabilities.
Management reiterated its long-term target of achieving an adjusted EBITDA margin of 28% by fiscal year 2026.
Veltora Systems was recognized by three independent industry analysts as a leader in the enterprise cloud infrastructure quadrant for 2023.
The internal audit function conducted reviews of 14 key business processes during fiscal year 2023 with no material weaknesses identified.
Marcus Delray expressed confidence that Veltora's product innovation pipeline would sustain competitive differentiation over the next five years.
The company's investor relations team hosted its annual Analyst Day on November 8, 2023, attended by over 200 institutional investors.
Veltora Systems remains committed to its environmental, social, and governance objectives, targeting carbon neutrality in its data center operations by 2028.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation, a mid-cap industrial automation company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, released its annual financial report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.
The company, founded in 2004, specializes in the design and manufacture of precision robotics systems for the automotive and aerospace supply chains.
Chief Executive Officer Patricia Holmgren opened the report with a message emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and operational resilience.
Chief Financial Officer Damon Xu noted that the fiscal year presented both significant headwinds and meaningful opportunities for the business.
Veltrano Dynamics reported total consolidated revenue of $1.84 billion for fiscal year 2023, representing a 9.3% increase compared to the prior year.
The automotive segment contributed $1.02 billion in revenue, while the aerospace segment added $820 million, reflecting strong demand across both verticals.
Gross profit for the year stood at $612 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 33.3%, up from 31.8% in fiscal year 2022.
Operating expenses, excluding cost of goods sold, totaled $388 million, resulting in operating income of $224 million for the full year.
Net income attributable to common shareholders came in at $163 million, translating to diluted earnings per share of $4.17.
The company's effective tax rate for fiscal year 2023 was 21.4%, in line with management's guidance range of 20% to 22%.
Adjusted EBITDA reached $310 million, representing an adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.8%, which exceeded the prior year figure of $271 million.
Capital expenditures for the year totaled $142 million, primarily directed toward expanding the Columbus manufacturing campus and upgrading robotic assembly lines.
Research and development spending increased by 14% year-over-year to $97 million, underscoring the company's commitment to next-generation automation platforms.
Veltrano Dynamics ended fiscal year 2023 with a total headcount of 8,740 employees, up from 8,210 at the end of 2022.
The workforce expansion was driven largely by hiring in the engineering and software integration divisions, where demand for skilled talent remained elevated.
Chief Operating Officer Sandra Pellegrini highlighted the successful ramp-up of the new Columbus South facility, which reached full production capacity in September 2023.
The Columbus South facility added approximately 220,000 square feet of manufacturing floor space, bringing the company's total footprint to roughly 1.1 million square feet.
Supply chain disruptions that had impacted the prior two fiscal years showed marked improvement, with component lead times returning to pre-pandemic norms by mid-year.
Inventory turnover improved to 6.2 times in fiscal year 2023, compared with 5.1 times in 2022, reflecting better procurement planning and lean manufacturing initiatives.
The company's order backlog at year-end stood at $2.1 billion, providing approximately 13.6 months of forward revenue coverage based on current run rates.
Veltrano Dynamics secured several landmark contracts during the year, including a five-year, $340 million agreement with a major North American automotive OEM.
The aerospace segment benefited from renewed defense spending trends and expanded its work with commercial aircraft manufacturers operating in the narrow-body market.
International revenue grew to $510 million, representing 27.7% of total revenue, driven by stronger performance in Germany, Japan, and South Korea.
The company's balance sheet reflected total assets of $2.63 billion and total liabilities of $1.41 billion as of December 31, 2023.
Veltrano Dynamics entered fiscal year 2024 carrying no long-term debt obligations, having fully retired its previous revolving credit facility in the third quarter of 2023.
Free cash flow generation for fiscal year 2023 was robust at $168 million, allowing the board to authorize a 15% increase in the quarterly dividend.
The quarterly dividend was raised to $0.46 per share, payable to shareholders of record as of January 15, 2024.
Share repurchases during the year totaled $85 million, reducing the weighted-average diluted share count by approximately 1.2 million shares.
The company's return on invested capital for fiscal year 2023 was 14.7%, comfortably above management's internal hurdle rate of 11%.
In February 2024, Veltrano Dynamics entered into a $450 million senior secured term loan facility with a syndicate of five financial institutions to fund strategic acquisitions.
Patricia Holmgren described the acquisition pipeline as the most compelling in the company's history, with several targets under active evaluation in the European automation market.
The company completed one bolt-on acquisition during fiscal year 2023, purchasing Stuttgart-based precision optics firm Kleiner Optik GmbH for approximately €62 million.
Kleiner Optik is expected to contribute roughly $28 million in annual revenue and be accretive to adjusted earnings per share within 12 months of closing.
Veltrano Dynamics maintained its investment-grade credit ratings, with Standard & Poor's affirming a BBB rating and Moody's maintaining a Baa2 designation.
The company disclosed no material litigation contingencies that would require accrual under ASC 450 as of the balance sheet date.
Environmental, social, and governance initiatives advanced meaningfully during the year, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions declining 8% on a revenue-intensity basis.
The company installed 4.2 megawatts of rooftop solar capacity across its manufacturing facilities, expected to offset approximately 6% of annual electricity consumption.
Veltrano Dynamics published its third annual sustainability report in November 2023, detailing progress against its 2030 emissions reduction roadmap.
Employee engagement scores, as measured by the annual internal survey, rose to 74% favorable, up four percentage points from the 2022 survey result.
The company experienced voluntary turnover of 8.3% in fiscal year 2023, down from 11.1% in the prior year, reflecting improved retention programs.
Chief People Officer Marcus Obi credited enhanced flexible work arrangements and a revised long-term incentive plan with improving employee satisfaction metrics.
Veltrano Dynamics launched two new product platforms during fiscal year 2023: the VX-9 collaborative robot series and the AeroPrecision 400 fixture alignment system.
The VX-9 series received certification from three major automotive OEMs and generated $41 million in first-year revenue, ahead of the internal forecast of $35 million.
The AeroPrecision 400 system was recognized with the industry's prestigious Automation Innovation Award at the 2023 Advanced Manufacturing Expo in Chicago.
Damon Xu provided fiscal year 2024 guidance, projecting revenue in the range of $1.96 billion to $2.02 billion, implying organic growth of 6.5% to 9.8%.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for fiscal year 2024 was set at $330 million to $345 million, suggesting continued margin expansion.
Capital expenditure guidance for 2024 was communicated at $120 million to $135 million, somewhat below the 2023 level as major facility investments wind down.
The company anticipates that free cash flow conversion will remain strong, targeting free cash flow of at least 50% of adjusted EBITDA.
Veltrano Dynamics plans to increase its R&D investment to approximately $110 million in fiscal year 2024, focusing on AI-enabled process automation and digital twin technologies.
Management indicated that the integration of Kleiner Optik GmbH is progressing ahead of schedule, with full systems integration expected to be completed by the second quarter of 2024.

Patricia Holmgren concluded the report by affirming the board's confidence in the company's long-term strategic direction, its talented workforce, and its competitive positioning in global industrial automation.
Veltrano Dynamics Corporation's shares trade on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol VLDR, and the company will host its annual shareholder meeting on April 22, 2024.
Veltara Biosystems, Inc. is a mid-cap biotechnology company headquartered in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina, specializing in targeted oncology therapeutics and companion diagnostic platforms.
Founded in 2009 by Dr. Miriam Okafor and Dr. Sebastien Leclaire, the company has grown from a university spinout into a commercially active biopharmaceutical enterprise.
This annual report covers the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023, and has been prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
Chief Executive Officer Dr. Priya Nambiar joined the company in 2018 and has since led a strategic pivot toward personalized medicine solutions.
Under her leadership, Veltara has expanded its pipeline from three candidate molecules to eleven, including two late-stage assets currently in Phase III trials.
Chief Financial Officer Marcus Hwang presented consolidated financial results to the Board of Directors on February 14, 2024.
Total revenue for fiscal year 2023 reached $418.7 million, representing a 22.4% increase compared to the $342.1 million recorded in fiscal year 2022.
Product revenue, derived primarily from sales of Veltara's approved therapy Karvenix, accounted for $361.2 million of total revenue.
Collaboration and licensing revenue contributed the remaining $57.5 million, driven largely by milestone payments from the company's partnership with Novess Pharmaceuticals.
Gross profit for the year was $294.3 million, yielding a gross margin of approximately 70.3%, consistent with the company's historical range.
Research and development expenses totaled $132.6 million in 2023, up from $109.4 million in 2022, reflecting accelerated investment in late-stage clinical programs.
Selling, general, and administrative expenses were $88.1 million, a modest increase of 6.2% from the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal year 2023 was $73.6 million, compared to $52.9 million in 2022, representing a 39.1% year-over-year improvement.
Net income attributable to common shareholders was $61.4 million, or $1.47 per diluted share.
This compares favorably to net income of $43.8 million, or $1.06 per diluted share, in fiscal year 2022.
Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure the company uses as a supplemental performance indicator, was $101.2 million for the full year.
Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2023 stood at $223.5 million, up from $178.9 million at year-end 2022.
The increase in cash was primarily attributable to strong operating cash flows of $94.7 million and net proceeds from the company's follow-on equity offering in March 2023.
In March 2023, Veltara completed a public offering of 4.2 million shares at $38.50 per share, generating gross proceeds of approximately $161.7 million.
Total assets at year-end were $876.4 million, including $189.7 million in intangible assets related to acquired in-process research programs.
Accounts receivable net of allowances was $74.3 million, reflecting strong collections performance with days sales outstanding of 37 days.
Inventory on hand was $42.1 million, sufficient to support approximately four months of forecasted Karvenix demand.
Capital expenditures for 2023 totaled $28.4 million, directed primarily toward expanding manufacturing capacity at the company's Durham, North Carolina facility.
The Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of up to $75 million in October 2023, of which $18.3 million had been utilized by year-end.
Veltara employed 1,284 full-time equivalent employees as of December 31, 2023, reflecting net headcount growth of 187 employees during the year.
The company's commercial team expanded by 64 representatives in 2023 to support the anticipated label extension for Karvenix in second-line non-small cell lung cancer.
Veltara's medical affairs and clinical operations functions added a combined 79 personnel to manage the increased complexity of the Phase III portfolio.
Chief Operating Officer Diana Ferreira highlighted continued improvements in manufacturing yield rates, which reached 94.2% in Q4 2023, up from 89.7% in Q4 2022.
The company's digital health subsidiary, Veltara Dx, recorded $12.4 million in standalone revenue from its companion diagnostic licensing agreements.
Dr. Nambiar noted in her letter to shareholders that disciplined capital allocation has been a defining theme of 2023 operations.
The company's long-term incentive program was restructured in 2023 to tie 60% of executive equity awards to total shareholder return relative to a peer group index.
Veltara's stock price appreciated 34.7% during fiscal year 2023, outperforming the NASDAQ Biotechnology Index by approximately 19 percentage points.
Looking ahead, the company provided initial fiscal year 2024 revenue guidance in the range of $470 million to $495 million.
Management expects gross margins to remain in the 69% to 72% range in 2024, supported by ongoing manufacturing efficiencies.
The company projects operating expenses will increase modestly as investment in the Karvenix label extension submission and two pipeline readouts accelerates in the first half of 2024.
As of December 31, 2023, Veltara carried no long-term debt on its balance sheet, having fully retired its $150 million term loan in September 2022.
Credit risk management has therefore focused exclusively on counterparty exposure in the company's investment portfolio rather than on servicing obligations.
The audit committee engaged PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the company's independent registered public accounting firm for the third consecutive year.
PricewaterhouseCoopers issued an unqualified audit opinion on the consolidated financial statements with no material weaknesses identified.
The company has maintained effective internal controls over financial reporting under Section 404(b) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act for five consecutive years.
In November 2023, Veltara entered into a $200 million revolving credit facility with a syndicate of lenders led by JPMorgan Chase, which was fully drawn at closing.
Interest expense recognized in the fourth quarter of 2023 totaled $3.1 million, reflecting costs associated with the company's new financing arrangement.
Management indicated that the proceeds were earmarked for potential business development transactions and pipeline acceleration activities.
Separately, the Board reviewed and reaffirmed the company's dividend policy, noting that Veltara has paid no cash dividends to common shareholders since inception.
The Board's position remains that reinvesting operating cash flows into internal R&D and selective acquisitions represents the highest-return capital allocation for shareholders.
Veltara's pipeline includes VLT-302, a KRAS G12C inhibitor in Phase III for colorectal cancer, with a primary endpoint readout expected in Q3 2024.
VLT-478, a next-generation PARP inhibitor for ovarian cancer, recently completed enrollment in its pivotal Phase III study ahead of schedule.
The companion diagnostic co-developed with Veltara Dx for VLT-302 received Breakthrough Device Designation from the FDA in August 2023.
Veltara's market capitalization exceeded $2 billion for the first time in company history during the third quarter of 2023.
The company holds 47 issued patents and 31 pending patent applications, with the core Karvenix composition-of-matter patent providing exclusivity through 2031.
Geographic expansion remains a strategic priority, with Veltara planning commercial launches in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom in the first half of 2024.
European regulatory submissions for Karvenix were accepted for review by the European Medicines Agency in October 2023.
Management expects European approval and first commercial sales in those markets no later than Q2 2024, subject to final pricing and reimbursement negotiations.
Dr. Nambiar closed her remarks by expressing confidence in Veltara's ability to deliver sustained value creation through a combination of organic growth and disciplined business development.
The Board and management team remain committed to transparency, operational excellence, and advancing the company's mission of improving outcomes for patients with difficult-to-treat cancers.
Veltara Systems Inc. is a mid-cap enterprise software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, specializing in cloud-based supply chain optimization platforms.
